contentBlocks.original:
नाम्नां साष्टसहस्रञ्च ब्रूहि गार्ग्य महामते ।
महालक्ष्म्या महादेव्या भुक्तिमुक्त्यर्थसिद्धये ॥ 1 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
nāmnāṃ sāṣṭasahasrañcha brūhi gārgya mahāmatē ।
mahālakṣmyā mahādēvyā bhuktimuktyarthasiddhayē ॥ 1 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: O Gargya, great-minded one — please tell us the eight-thousand names of the great goddess Mahalakshmi, for the attainment of worldly enjoyment and liberation. (1)

contentBlocks.original:
गार्ग्य उवाच
सनत्कुमारमासीनं द्वादशादित्यसन्निभम् ।
अपृच्छन्योगिनो भक्त्या योगिनामर्थसिद्धये ॥ 2 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
gārgya uvācha
sanatkumāramāsīnaṃ dvādaśādityasannibham ।
apṛchChanyōginō bhaktyā yōgināmarthasiddhayē ॥ 2 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Gargya said: The yogis, seeking the welfare of all yogis, approached Sanatkumara — who was seated in meditation, radiant as twelve suns — and asked him with devotion. (2)

contentBlocks.original:
सर्वलौकिककर्मभ्यो विमुक्तानां हिताय वै ।
भुक्तिमुक्तिप्रदं जप्यमनुब्रूहि दयानिधे ॥ 3 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
sarvalaukikakarmabhyō vimuktānāṃ hitāya vai ।
bhuktimuktipradaṃ japyamanubrūhi dayānidhē ॥ 3 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: For the benefit of those freed from all worldly duties, please teach us the chant that bestows both enjoyment and liberation. O ocean of compassion, reveal it to us. (3)

contentBlocks.original:
सनत्कुमार भगवन्सर्वज्ञोऽसि विशेषतः ।
आस्तिक्यसिद्धये नॄणां क्षिप्रधर्मार्थसाधनम् ॥ 4 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
sanatkumāra bhagavansarvajñō'si viśēṣataḥ ।
āstikyasiddhayē nṝṇāṃ kṣipradharmārthasādhanam ॥ 4 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: O Sanatkumara, O blessed one, you are all-knowing in a special way. Please quickly set forth the means for attaining righteous duty and wealth for the confirmation of faith among men. (4)

contentBlocks.original:
खिद्यन्ति मानवास्सर्वे धनाभावेन केवलम् ।
सिद्ध्यन्ति धनिनोऽन्यस्य नैव धर्मार्थकामनाः ॥ 5 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
khidyanti mānavāssarvē dhanābhāvēna kēvalam ।
siddhyanti dhaninō'nyasya naiva dharmārthakāmanāḥ ॥ 5 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: All human beings suffer from poverty alone. For those who possess wealth, the objects of righteousness, wealth, and desire do not fail to be fulfilled — but for the poor they do not succeed. (5)

contentBlocks.original:
दारिद्र्यध्वंसिनी नाम केन विद्या प्रकीर्तिता ।
केन वा ब्रह्मविद्याऽपि केन मृत्युविनाशिनी ॥ 6 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
dāridryadhvaṃsinī nāma kēna vidyā prakīrtitā ।
kēna vā brahmavidyā'pi kēna mṛtyuvināśinī ॥ 6 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: By whom was the knowledge called "destroyer of poverty" proclaimed? By whom was the knowledge of Brahman declared? By whom was the destroyer of death made known? (6)

contentBlocks.original:
सर्वासां सारभूतैका विद्यानां केन कीर्तिता ।
प्रत्यक्षसिद्धिदा ब्रह्मन् तामाचक्ष्व दयानिधे ॥ 7 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
sarvāsāṃ sārabhūtaikā vidyānāṃ kēna kīrtitā ।
pratyakṣasiddhidā brahman tāmāchakṣva dayānidhē ॥ 7 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: What single knowledge is the essence of all knowledges? O Brahmin, O ocean of compassion — what knowledge gives direct results? Please declare it to us. (7)

contentBlocks.original:
सनत्कुमार उवाच
साधु पृष्टं महाभागास्सर्वलोकहितैषिणः ।
महतामेष धर्मश्च नान्येषामिति मे मतिः ॥ 8 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
sanatkumāra uvācha
sādhu pṛṣṭaṃ mahābhāgāssarvalōkahitaiṣiṇaḥ ।
mahatāmēṣa dharmaścha nānyēṣāmiti mē matiḥ ॥ 8 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Sanatkumara said: Well asked, O greatly fortunate ones who seek the welfare of all worlds. This indeed is the dharma of the great — not of others: such is my conviction. (8)

contentBlocks.original:
ब्रह्मविष्णुमहादेवमहेन्द्रादिमहात्मभिः ।
सम्प्रोक्तं कथयाम्यद्य लक्ष्मीनामसहस्रकम् ॥ 9 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
brahmaviṣṇumahādēvamahēndrādimahātmabhiḥ ।
samprōktaṃ kathayāmyadya lakṣmīnāmasahasrakam ॥ 9 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: I shall today recount the thousand names of Lakshmi, as proclaimed by Brahma, Vishnu, Maheshvara, Mahendra, and other great souls. (9)

contentBlocks.original:
यस्योच्चारणमात्रेण दारिद्र्यान्मुच्यते नरः ।
किं पुनस्तज्जपाज्जापी सर्वेष्टार्थानवाप्नुयात् ॥ 10 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
yasyōchchāraṇamātrēṇa dāridryānmuchyatē naraḥ ।
kiṃ punastajjapājjāpī sarvēṣṭārthānavāpnuyāt ॥ 10 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: By the mere utterance of these names, a man is freed from poverty. What more need be said — one who regularly chants them with devotion shall attain all desired objects. (10)

contentBlocks.original: अस्य श्रीलक्ष्मीदिव्यसहस्रनामस्तोत्रमहामन्त्रस्य आनन्दकर्दमचिक्लीतेन्दिरासुतादयो महात्मानो महर्षयः अनुष्टुप्छन्दः विष्णुमाया शक्तिः महालक्ष्मीः परादेवता श्रीमहालक्ष्मीप्रसादद्वारा सर्वेष्टार्थसिद्ध्यर्थे जपे विनियोगः ।
contentBlocks.transliteration: asya śrīlakṣmīdivyasahasranāmastōtramahāmantrasya ānandakardamachiklītēndirāsutādayō mahātmānō maharṣayaḥ anuṣṭupChandaḥ viṣṇumāyā śaktiḥ mahālakṣmīḥ parādēvatā śrīmahālakṣmīprasādadvārā sarvēṣṭārthasiddhyarthē japē viniyōgaḥ ।
contentBlocks.translation: Invocation (Viniyoga): Of this great mantra, the Sri Lakshmi Divine Thousand-Names Stotra, the seers are Ananda, Kardama, Chiklitendrira, and their sons; the metre is Anushtup; the power (shakti) is Vishnu Maya; the presiding deity is Mahalakshmi; it is employed for the recitation that bestows all desired objects through the grace of Sri Mahalakshmi.

contentBlocks.original:
ध्यानम्
पद्मनाभप्रियां देवीं पद्माक्षीं पद्मवासिनीम् ।
पद्मवक्त्रां पद्महस्तां वन्दे पद्मामहर्निशम् ॥ 1 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
dhyānam
padmanābhapriyāṃ dēvīṃ padmākṣīṃ padmavāsinīm ।
padmavaktrāṃ padmahastāṃ vandē padmāmaharniśam ॥ 1 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Dhyana (meditation): I worship day and night the lotus-goddess — she who is dear to Padmanabha (Vishnu), lotus-eyed, dwelling in the lotus, lotus-faced, lotus-handed — the resplendent Padma. (1)

contentBlocks.original:
पूर्णेन्दुवदनां दिव्यरत्नाभरणभूषिताम् ।
वरदाभयहस्ताढ्यां ध्यायेच्चन्द्रसहोदरीम् ॥ 2 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
pūrṇēnduvadanāṃ divyaratnābharaṇabhūṣitām ।
varadābhayahastāḍhyāṃ dhyāyēchchandrasahōdarīm ॥ 2 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Dhyana (continued): Meditate upon she whose face is like the full moon, adorned with divine jewelled ornaments, with hands displaying the gestures of boon and protection — the sister of the moon. (2)

contentBlocks.original:
इच्छारूपां भगवतस्सच्चिदानन्दरूपिणीम् ।
सर्वज्ञां सर्वजननीं विष्णुवक्षस्स्थलालयाम् ।
दयालुमनिशं ध्यायेत्सुखसिद्धिस्वरूपिणीम् ॥ 3 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
ichChārūpāṃ bhagavatassachchidānandarūpiṇīm ।
sarvajñāṃ sarvajananīṃ viṣṇuvakṣassthalālayām ।
dayālumaniśaṃ dhyāyētsukhasiddhisvarūpiṇīm ॥ 3 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Dhyana (continued): She who assumes the form desired by the Lord, whose nature is pure being-consciousness-bliss, who is all-knowing and the mother of all, who dwells on the chest of Vishnu — meditate ceaselessly on her, the very form of bliss and perfection. (3)

contentBlocks.original:
स्तोत्रम्
नित्यागतानन्तनित्या नन्दिनी जनरञ्जनी ।
नित्यप्रकाशिनी चैव स्वप्रकाशस्वरूपिणी ॥ 1 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
stōtram
nityāgatānantanityā nandinī janarañjanī ।
nityaprakāśinī chaiva svaprakāśasvarūpiṇī ॥ 1 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Nitya (the Eternal), Agata (the Arrived), Ananta (the Boundless), Nitya (the Ever-present), Nandini (the Blissful), Janaranjanī (Delighter of people), Nityaprakashinī (Eternally luminous), Svaprakashasvarupinī (whose nature is self-luminous). (1)

contentBlocks.original:
महालक्ष्मीर्महाकाली महाकन्या सरस्वती ।
भोगवैभवसन्धात्री भक्तानुग्रहकारिणी ॥ 2 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
mahālakṣmīrmahākālī mahākanyā sarasvatī ।
bhōgavaibhavasandhātrī bhaktānugrahakāriṇī ॥ 2 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Mahalakshmi (great Lakshmi), Mahakali (great Kali), Mahakanya (great maiden), Sarasvati (goddess of learning), Bhogavaibhavasandhātrī (bestower of wealth and splendour), Bhaktanugrahakārinī (bestower of grace upon devotees). (2)

contentBlocks.original:
ईशावास्या महामाया महादेवी महेश्वरी ।
हृल्लेखा परमा शक्तिर्मातृकाबीजरूपिणी ॥ 3 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
īśāvāsyā mahāmāyā mahādēvī mahēśvarī ।
hṛllēkhā paramā śaktirmātṛkābījarūpiṇī ॥ 3 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Ishavāsyā (pervading all), Mahamaya (great illusion), Mahadevi (great goddess), Maheshvari (great sovereign), Hrillekhā (the seed-syllable Hrim), Parashakti (supreme power), Matrikabījarūpinī (embodied as the seed of the matrikas). (3)

contentBlocks.original:
नित्यानन्दा नित्यबोधा नादिनी जनमोदिनी ।
सत्यप्रत्ययनी चैव स्वप्रकाशात्मरूपिणी ॥ 4 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
nityānandā nityabōdhā nādinī janamōdinī ।
satyapratyayanī chaiva svaprakāśātmarūpiṇī ॥ 4 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Nityananda (eternal bliss), Nityabodha (eternal awareness), Nadinī (flowing like a river), Janamodinī (joy of the people), Satyapratyayanī (truth-affirming), Svaprakashatmarūpinī (whose self-nature is self-luminous). (4)

contentBlocks.original:
त्रिपुरा भैरवी विद्या हंसा वागीश्वरी शिवा ।
वाग्देवी च महारात्रिः कालरात्रिस्त्रिलोचना ॥ 5 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
tripurā bhairavī vidyā haṃsā vāgīśvarī śivā ।
vāgdēvī cha mahārātriḥ kālarātristrilōchanā ॥ 5 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Tripura (triple-city goddess), Bhairavi (the fierce one), Vidya (knowledge), Hamsa (the swan/mantra), Vagishvari (mistress of speech), Shiva (auspicious), Vagdevi (speech-goddess), Maharatri (great night), Kalaratri (night of dissolution), Trilochanā (three-eyed). (5)

contentBlocks.original:
भद्रकाली कराली च महाकाली तिलोत्तमा ।
काली करालवक्त्रान्ता कामाक्षी कामदा शुभा ॥ 6 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
bhadrakāḻī karāḻī cha mahākāḻī tilōttamā ।
kāḻī karāḻavaktrāntā kāmākṣī kāmadā śubhā ॥ 6 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Bhadrakali (auspicious Kali), Karali (the fierce), Mahakali (great Kali), Tilottama (most excellent), Kali (the dark one), Karalavaktranīta (with gaping dreadful face), Kamakshi (desire-eyed), Kamada (wish-fulfiller), Shubha (auspicious). (6)

contentBlocks.original:
चण्डिका चण्डरूपेशा चामुण्डा चक्रधारिणी ।
त्रैलोक्यजयिनी देवी त्रैलोक्यविजयोत्तमा ॥ 7 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
chaṇḍikā chaṇḍarūpēśā chāmuṇḍā chakradhāriṇī ।
trailōkyajayinī dēvī trailōkyavijayōttamā ॥ 7 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Chandika (the fierce), Chandarupeshā (sovereign of fierce forms), Chamunda (slayer of Chanda-Munda), Chakradharinī (discus-bearing), Trailokya-jayini (conqueror of the three worlds), Devi (goddess), Trailokya-vijayottama (supreme victor of the three worlds). (7)

contentBlocks.original:
सिद्धलक्ष्मीः क्रियालक्ष्मीर्मोक्षलक्ष्मीः प्रसादिनी ।
उमा भगवती दुर्गा चान्द्री दाक्षायणी शिवा ॥ 8 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
siddhalakṣmīḥ kriyālakṣmīrmōkṣalakṣmīḥ prasādinī ।
umā bhagavatī durgā chāndrī dākṣāyaṇī śivā ॥ 8 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Siddhalakshmi (perfection-Lakshmi), Kriyalakshmi (action-Lakshmi), Mokshalakshmi (liberation-Lakshmi), Prasadinī (the gracious), Uma (Parvati), Bhagavati (blessed goddess), Durga (the fortress), Chandri (moon-goddess), Dakshayani (daughter of Daksha), Shiva (auspicious). (8)

contentBlocks.original:
प्रत्यङ्गिरा धरावेला लोकमाता हरिप्रिया ।
पार्वती परमा देवी ब्रह्मविद्याप्रदायिनी ॥ 9 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
pratyaṅgirā dharāvēlā lōkamātā haripriyā ।
pārvatī paramā dēvī brahmavidyāpradāyinī ॥ 9 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Pratyangira (averting goddess), Dharavela (tide of the earth), Lokamata (mother of the world), Haripriya (beloved of Hari), Parvati (mountain daughter), Parama (supreme), Devi (goddess), Brahmavidyapradayinī (bestower of Brahma-knowledge). (9)

contentBlocks.original:
अरूपा बहुरूपा च विरूपा विश्वरूपिणी ।
पञ्चभूतात्मिका वाणी पञ्चभूतात्मिका परा ॥ 10 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
arūpā bahurūpā cha virūpā viśvarūpiṇī ।
pañchabhūtātmikā vāṇī pañchabhūtātmikā parā ॥ 10 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Arupa (formless), Bahurupa (many-formed), Virupa (strange-formed), Vishvarupinī (universe-formed), Panchabhutatmika (essence of the five elements), Vani (speech), Panchabhutatmika Para (supreme essence of the five elements). (10)

contentBlocks.original:
काली मा पञ्चिका वाग्मी हविःप्रत्यधिदेवता ।
देवमाता सुरेशाना देवगर्भाऽम्बिका धृतिः ॥ 11 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
kāḻī mā pañchikā vāgmī haviḥpratyadhidēvatā ।
dēvamātā surēśānā dēvagarbhā'mbikā dhṛtiḥ ॥ 11 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Kali Ma (the dark mother), Panchika (fivefold), Vagmi (eloquent), Havis-praty-adhidevata (presiding deity of oblations), Devamata (mother of gods), Sureshanā (sovereign of gods), Devagarbha (womb of gods), Ambika (the mother), Dhriti (fortitude). (11)

contentBlocks.original:
सङ्ख्या जातिः क्रियाशक्तिः प्रकृतिर्मोहिनी मही ।
यज्ञविद्या महाविद्या गुह्यविद्या विभावरी ॥ 12 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
saṅkhyā jātiḥ kriyāśaktiḥ prakṛtirmōhinī mahī ।
yajñavidyā mahāvidyā guhyavidyā vibhāvarī ॥ 12 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Sankhya (enumeration), Jati (birth/category), Kriyashakti (power of action), Prakriti (primordial nature), Mohini (enchantress), Mahi (the earth), Yajnavidya (knowledge of sacrifice), Mahavidya (great knowledge), Guhyavidya (secret knowledge), Vibhavari (the radiant night). (12)

contentBlocks.original:
ज्योतिष्मती महामाता सर्वमन्त्रफलप्रदा ।
दारिद्र्यध्वंसिनी देवी हृदयग्रन्थिभेदिनी ॥ 13 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
jyōtiṣmatī mahāmātā sarvamantraphalapradā ।
dāridryadhvaṃsinī dēvī hṛdayagranthibhēdinī ॥ 13 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Jyotishmati (luminous), Mahamata (great mother), Sarvamantraphalaprada (giver of the fruits of all mantras), Daridryadvamshinī Devi (goddess who destroys poverty), Hridayagranthibhedinī (she who breaks the knot in the heart). (13)

contentBlocks.original:
सहस्रादित्यसङ्काशा चन्द्रिका चन्द्ररूपिणी ।
गायत्री सोमसम्भूतिस्सावित्री प्रणवात्मिका ॥ 14 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
sahasrādityasaṅkāśā chandrikā chandrarūpiṇī ।
gāyatrī sōmasambhūtissāvitrī praṇavātmikā ॥ 14 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Sahasradityasankasha (radiant as a thousand suns), Chandrika (moonbeam), Chandrarupinī (moon-formed), Gayatri (the sacred meter/hymn), Somasambhuti (born of the soma moon), Savitri (the sun-goddess), Pranava-tmika (embodied as Om). (14)

contentBlocks.original:
शाङ्करी वैष्णवी ब्राह्मी सर्वदेवनमस्कृता ।
सेव्यदुर्गा कुबेराक्षी करवीरनिवासिनी ॥ 15 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
śāṅkarī vaiṣṇavī brāhmī sarvadēvanamaskṛtā ।
sēvyadurgā kubērākṣī karavīranivāsinī ॥ 15 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Shankari (Shiva's consort), Vaishnavi (Vishnu's power), Brahmi (Brahma's power), Sarvadevanamaskhrita (worshipped by all gods), Sevyadurga (Durga who is served), Kuberakshi (Kubera-eyed), Karavira-nivasinī (dwelling in the oleander grove). (15)

contentBlocks.original:
जया च विजया चैव जयन्ती चाऽपराजिता ।
कुब्जिका कालिका शास्त्री वीणापुस्तकधारिणी ॥ 16 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
jayā cha vijayā chaiva jayantī chā'parājitā ।
kubjikā kālikā śāstrī vīṇāpustakadhāriṇī ॥ 16 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Jaya (victory), Vijaya (conquest), Jayanti (ever-victorious), Aparajita (undefeated), Kubjika (the hunchbacked goddess), Kalika (Kali), Shastri (the teacher), Vinapustakadhārinī (holding the veena and book). (16)

contentBlocks.original:
सर्वज्ञशक्तिश्श्रीशक्तिर्ब्रह्मविष्णुशिवात्मिका ।
इडापिङ्गलिकामध्यमृणालीतन्तुरूपिणी ॥ 17 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
sarvajñaśaktiśśrīśaktirbrahmaviṣṇuśivātmikā ।
iḍāpiṅgalikāmadhyamṛṇālītanturūpiṇī ॥ 17 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Sarvajna-shakti (power of omniscience), Shri-shakti (power of Shri), Brahma-Vishnu-Shivatmika (embodied as Brahma, Vishnu, and Shiva), Ida-Pingalika-madhya (between the Ida and Pingala nadis), Mrinali-tantu-rupinī (like the fibre-thread of a lotus stalk). (17)

contentBlocks.original:
यज्ञेशानी प्रथा दीक्षा दक्षिणा सर्वमोहिनी ।
अष्टाङ्गयोगिनी देवी निर्बीजध्यानगोचरा ॥ 18 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
yajñēśānī prathā dīkṣā dakṣiṇā sarvamōhinī ।
aṣṭāṅgayōginī dēvī nirbījadhyānagōcharā ॥ 18 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Yajneshani (mistress of sacrifice), Pratha (tradition), Diksha (initiation), Dakshina (the sacred fee/south), Sarvamohini (enchantress of all), Ashtangayoginī (eight-limbed yogini), Devi (goddess), Nirbijadhyanagochara (accessible through seedless meditation). (18)

contentBlocks.original:
सर्वतीर्थस्थिता शुद्धा सर्वपर्वतवासिनी ।
वेदशास्त्रप्रभा देवी षडङ्गादिपदक्रमा ॥ 19 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
sarvatīrthasthitā śuddhā sarvaparvatavāsinī ।
vēdaśāstraprabhā dēvī ṣaḍaṅgādipadakramā ॥ 19 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Sarvatirthasthita (present in all sacred fords), Shuddha (pure), Sarvaparvatavasinī (dwelling on all mountains), Vedashastraprabha (radiance of Veda and scripture), Devi (goddess), Shadangadipadakrama (following the sequence of the six Vedic limbs). (19)

contentBlocks.original:
शिवा धात्री शुभानन्दा यज्ञकर्मस्वरूपिणी ।
व्रतिनी मेनका देवी ब्रह्माणी ब्रह्मचारिणी ॥ 20 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
śivā dhātrī śubhānandā yajñakarmasvarūpiṇī ।
vratinī mēnakā dēvī brahmāṇī brahmachāriṇī ॥ 20 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Shiva (auspicious), Dhatri (nurturer), Shubhananda (auspicious joy), Yajnakarmasvarupinī (whose form is the action of sacrifice), Vratini (the vow-keeper), Menaka (the celestial nymph), Devi (goddess), Brahmani (Brahma's consort), Brahmacharinī (celibate ascetic). (20)

contentBlocks.original:
एकाक्षरपरा तारा भवबन्धविनाशिनी ।
विश्वम्भरा धराधारा निराधाराऽधिकस्वरा ॥ 21 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
ēkākṣaraparā tārā bhavabandhavināśinī ।
viśvambharā dharādhārā nirādhārā'dhikasvarā ॥ 21 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Ekakshara-para (supreme single-syllable), Tara (the deliverer), Bhavabandha-vinashinī (destroyer of the bondage of existence), Vishvambhara (sustainer of the universe), Dharadhara (upholder of the earth), Niradhara (without support), Adhikasvarā (of supreme sound). (21)

contentBlocks.original:
राका कुहूरमावास्या पूर्णिमाऽनुमतिर्द्युतिः ।
सिनीवाली शिवाऽवश्या वैश्वदेवी पिशङ्गिला ॥ 22 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
rākā kuhūramāvāsyā pūrṇimā'numatirdyutiḥ ।
sinīvālī śivā'vaśyā vaiśvadēvī piśaṅgilā ॥ 22 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Raka (full-moon night), Kuhu (new-moon night), Amavasya (new-moon day), Purnima (full-moon day), Anumati (consent/lunar day), Dyuti (radiance), Sinivali (day before new moon), Shiva (auspicious), Avashya (inevitable), Vaishvadevi (goddess of all), Pishangila (tawny). (22)

contentBlocks.original:
पिप्पला च विशालाक्षी रक्षोघ्नी वृष्टिकारिणी ।
दुष्टविद्राविणी देवी सर्वोपद्रवनाशिनी ॥ 23 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
pippalā cha viśālākṣī rakṣōghnī vṛṣṭikāriṇī ।
duṣṭavidrāviṇī dēvī sarvōpadravanāśinī ॥ 23 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Pippala (sacred fig), Vishalakshi (wide-eyed), Rakshoghnī (destroyer of demons), Vrishtikārinī (bringer of rain), Dushtavidrāvinī (scatterer of the wicked), Devi (goddess), Sarvopadravanashinī (destroyer of all calamities). (23)

contentBlocks.original:
शारदा शरसन्धाना सर्वशस्त्रस्वरूपिणी ।
युद्धमध्यस्थिता देवी सर्वभूतप्रभञ्जनी ॥ 24 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
śāradā śarasandhānā sarvaśastrasvarūpiṇī ।
yuddhamadhyasthitā dēvī sarvabhūtaprabhañjanī ॥ 24 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Sharada (goddess of learning/autumn), Sharasandhana (stringing arrows), Sarvashastra-svarupinī (whose form is all weapons), Yuddhamadhyasthita (standing in the midst of battle), Devi (goddess), Sarvabhuta-prabhanjani (wind that shatters all beings). (24)

contentBlocks.original:
अयुद्धा युद्धरूपा च शान्ता शान्तिस्वरूपिणी ।
गङ्गा सरस्वतीवेणीयमुनानर्मदापगा ॥ 25 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
ayuddhā yuddharūpā cha śāntā śāntisvarūpiṇī ।
gaṅgā sarasvatīvēṇīyamunānarmadāpagā ॥ 25 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Ayuddha (without weapons), Yuddharupa (battle-formed), Shanta (peaceful), Shanti-svarupinī (whose nature is peace), Ganga, Sarasvati, Veni, Yamuna, Narmada, Apaga (the river banks) — she is all the sacred rivers. (25)

contentBlocks.original:
समुद्रवसनावासा ब्रह्माण्डश्रोणिमेखला ।
पञ्चवक्त्रा दशभुजा शुद्धस्फटिकसन्निभा ॥ 26 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
samudravasanāvāsā brahmāṇḍaśrōṇimēkhalā ।
pañchavaktrā daśabhujā śuddhasphaṭikasannibhā ॥ 26 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Samudravasana (ocean-robed), Vasa (dwelling), Brahmandashronimekhalā (whose girdle is the cosmic egg's equator), Panchavaktra (five-faced), Dashabhuja (ten-armed), Shuddha-sphatikasamniabha (pure as crystal). (26)

contentBlocks.original:
रक्ता कृष्णा सिता पीता सर्ववर्णा निरीश्वरी ।
कालिका चक्रिका देवी सत्या तु वटुकास्थिता ॥ 27 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
raktā kṛṣṇā sitā pītā sarvavarṇā nirīśvarī ।
kāḻikā chakrikā dēvī satyā tu vaṭukāsthitā ॥ 27 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Rakta (red), Krishna (dark), Sita (white), Pita (yellow), Sarvavarṇā (of all colours), Nirishvari (without a lord), Kalika (Kali), Chakrika (discus-bearer), Devi (goddess), Satya (truth), Vatuka-sthita (dwelling in Vatuka). (27)

contentBlocks.original:
तरुणी वारुणी नारी ज्येष्ठादेवी सुरेश्वरी ।
विश्वम्भराधरा कर्त्री गलार्गलविभञ्जनी ॥ 28 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
taruṇī vāruṇī nārī jyēṣṭhādēvī surēśvarī ।
viśvambharādharā kartrī gaḻārgaḻavibhañjanī ॥ 28 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Taruni (the young one), Varuni (Varuna's goddess), Nari (woman), Jyeshtha-devi (eldest goddess), Sureshvari (sovereign of gods), Vishvambhara-dhara (bearer of the earth's weight), Kartri (the doer), Galargala-vibhanjani (breaker of the throat-locks). (28)

contentBlocks.original:
सन्ध्यारात्रिर्दिवाज्योत्स्ना कलाकाष्ठा निमेषिका ।
उर्वी कात्यायनी शुभ्रा संसारार्णवतारिणी ॥ 29 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
sandhyārātrirdivājyōtsnā kalākāṣṭhā nimēṣikā ।
urvī kātyāyanī śubhrā saṃsārārṇavatāriṇī ॥ 29 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Sandhyaratri (twilight night), Diva (day), Jyotsna (moonlight), Kalakashtha (unit of time), Nimeshika (the blink of an eye), Urvi (the wide earth), Katyayani (the warrior goddess), Shubhra (brilliant), Samsararnava-tarinī (she who carries us across the ocean of existence). (29)

contentBlocks.original:
कपिला कीलिकाऽशोका मल्लिकानवमल्लिका । [ मल्लिकानवमालिका ]
देविका नन्दिका शान्ता भञ्जिका भयभञ्जिका ॥ 30 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
kapilā kīlikā'śōkā mallikānavamallikā । [ mallikānavamālikā ]
dēvikā nandikā śāntā bhañjikā bhayabhañjikā ॥ 30 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Kapila (tawny), Kilika (protective), Ashoka (sorrowless), Mallikanavamallika (jasmine-garlanded), Devika (little goddess), Nandika (joyful), Shanta (peaceful), Bhanjika (breaker), Bhaya-bhanjika (breaker of fear). (30)

contentBlocks.original:
कौशिकी वैदिकी देवी सौरी रूपाधिकाऽतिभा ।
दिग्वस्त्रा नववस्त्रा च कन्यका कमलोद्भवा ॥ 31 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
kauśikī vaidikī dēvī saurī rūpādhikā'tibhā ।
digvastrā navavastrā cha kanyakā kamalōdbhavā ॥ 31 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Kaushiki (sheath-born), Vaidiki (Vedic), Devi (goddess), Sauri (solar), Rupadhika (surpassing beauty), Atibha (super-radiant), Digvasthra (sky-clad), Navavasthra (newly clad), Kanyaka (maiden), Kamalod-bhava (lotus-born). (31)

contentBlocks.original:
श्रीस्सौम्यलक्षणाऽतीतदुर्गा सूत्रप्रबोधिका ।
श्रद्धा मेधा कृतिः प्रज्ञा धारणा कान्तिरेव च ॥ 32 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
śrīssaumyalakṣaṇā'tītadurgā sūtraprabōdhikā ।
śraddhā mēdhā kṛtiḥ prajñā dhāraṇā kāntirēva cha ॥ 32 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Shri (auspiciousness), Saumyalakshana (with auspicious marks), Atita-durga (beyond hardship), Sutraprabodhika (awakener of the thread), Shraddha (faith), Medha (intelligence), Kriti (creativity), Prajna (wisdom), Dharana (retention), Kanti (radiance). (32)

contentBlocks.original:
श्रुतिः स्मृतिर्धृतिर्धन्या भूतिरिष्टिर्मनीषिणी ।
विरक्तिर्व्यापिनी माया सर्वमायाप्रभञ्जनी ॥ 33 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
śrutiḥ smṛtirdhṛtirdhanyā bhūtiriṣṭirmanīṣiṇī ।
viraktirvyāpinī māyā sarvamāyāprabhañjanī ॥ 33 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Shruti (Vedic revelation), Smriti (tradition), Dhriti (steadiness), Dhanya (the blessed), Bhuti (prosperity), Ishti (desire), Manishinī (the wise), Virakti (dispassion), Vyapinī (all-pervading), Maya (illusion), Sarvamaya-prabhanjani (destroyer of all illusion). (33)

contentBlocks.original:
माहेन्द्री मन्त्रिणी सिंही चेन्द्रजालस्वरूपिणी ।
अवस्थात्रयनिर्मुक्ता गुणत्रयविवर्जिता ॥ 34 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
māhēndrī mantriṇī siṃhī chēndrajālasvarūpiṇī ।
avasthātrayanirmuktā guṇatrayavivarjitā ॥ 34 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Mahendri (Indra's consort), Mantrini (the minister-goddess), Simhi (lion-riding), Chendrajala-svarupinī (whose form is the magic of Indra), Avastha-trayanirmukta (free from the three states), Gunatrayavivarjita (beyond the three gunas). (34)

contentBlocks.original:
ईषणात्रयनिर्मुक्ता सर्वरोगविवर्जिता ।
योगिध्यानान्तगम्या च योगध्यानपरायणा ॥ 35 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
īṣaṇātrayanirmuktā sarvarōgavivarjitā ।
yōgidhyānāntagamyā cha yōgadhyānaparāyaṇā ॥ 35 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Ishanatrayanimukta (free from the three desires), Sarvarogavivarjita (free from all disease), Yogidhyananta-gamya (attainable at the end of yogic meditation), Yogadhyana-parayana (devoted to yogic meditation). (35)

contentBlocks.original:
त्रयीशिखा विशेषज्ञा वेदान्तज्ञानरूपिणी ।
भारती कमला भाषा पद्मा पद्मवती कृतिः ॥ 36 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
trayīśikhā viśēṣajñā vēdāntajñānarūpiṇī ।
bhāratī kamalā bhāṣā padmā padmavatī kṛtiḥ ॥ 36 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Trayishikha (crown of the triple Veda), Visheshajña (knower of particulars), Vedantajñana-rupinī (whose form is Vedantic knowledge), Bharati (goddess of speech), Kamala (lotus goddess), Bhasha (language), Padma (lotus-seated), Padmavati (lotus-dwelling), Kriti (creative power). (36)

contentBlocks.original:
गौतमी गोमती गौरी ईशाना हंसवाहिनी ।
नारायणी प्रभाधारा जाह्नवी शङ्करात्मजा ॥ 37 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
gautamī gōmatī gaurī īśānā haṃsavāhinī ।
nārāyaṇī prabhādhārā jāhnavī śaṅkarātmajā ॥ 37 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Gautami (sage Gautama's tradition), Gomati (cow-rich), Gauri (the fair), Ishana (the ruling one), Hamsavahini (swan-mounted), Narayani (Narayana's power), Prabhadhara (bearer of radiance), Jahnavi (Ganga), Shankaratmaja (Shankara's daughter). (37)

contentBlocks.original:
चित्रघण्टा सुनन्दा श्रीर्मानवी मनुसम्भवा ।
स्तम्भिनी क्षोभिणी मारी भ्रामिणी शत्रुमारिणी ॥ 38 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
chitraghaṇṭā sunandā śrīrmānavī manusambhavā ।
stambhinī kṣōbhiṇī mārī bhrāmiṇī śatrumāriṇī ॥ 38 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Chitra-ghanta (picture-bell), Sunanda (auspicious joy), Shri (glory), Manavi (mind-born), Manu-sambhava (born of Manu), Stambhini (paralyzer), Kshobhini (agitator), Mari (the plague), Bhramini (she who bewilders), Shatru-marini (slayer of enemies). (38)

contentBlocks.original:
मोहिनी द्वेषिणी वीरा अघोरा रुद्ररूपिणी ।
रुद्रैकादशिनी पुण्या कल्याणी लाभकारिणी ॥ 39 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
mōhinī dvēṣiṇī vīrā aghōrā rudrarūpiṇī ।
rudraikādaśinī puṇyā kalyāṇī lābhakāriṇī ॥ 39 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Mohini (enchantress), Dveshini (repeller of hatred), Vira (heroic), Aghora (the non-fierce/fierce), Rudrarupini (Rudra-formed), Rudra-ekadashini (of the eleven Rudras), Punya (meritorious), Kalyani (the auspicious), Labhakārinī (bringer of gain). (39)

contentBlocks.original:
देवदुर्गा महादुर्गा स्वप्नदुर्गाऽष्टभैरवी ।
सूर्यचन्द्राग्निरूपा च ग्रहनक्षत्ररूपिणी ॥ 40 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
dēvadurgā mahādurgā svapnadurgā'ṣṭabhairavī ।
sūryachandrāgnirūpā cha grahanakṣatrarūpiṇī ॥ 40 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Deva-durga (divine Durga), Maha-durga (great Durga), Svapna-durga (dream Durga), Ashta-bhairavi (eight Bhairavis), Surya-chandra-agnirupacha (form of sun, moon, and fire), Graha-nakshatra-rupinī (formed of planets and stars). (40)

contentBlocks.original:
बिन्दुनादकलातीता बिन्दुनादकलात्मिका ।
दशवायुजयाकारा कलाषोडशसंयुता ॥ 41 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
bindunādakaḻātītā bindunādakaḻātmikā ।
daśavāyujayākārā kaḻāṣōḍaśasaṃyutā ॥ 41 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Bindu-nada-kalatita (beyond bindu, nada, and kala), Bindu-nada-kalatmika (essence of bindu, nada, and kala), Dashavayujayakara (assuming victory over the ten vital breaths), Kalashodasasamyuta (united with the sixteen lunar digits). (41)

contentBlocks.original:
काश्यपी कमलादेवी नादचक्रनिवासिनी ।
मृडाधारा स्थिरा गुह्या देविका चक्ररूपिणी ॥ 42 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
kāśyapī kamalādēvī nādachakranivāsinī ।
mṛḍādhārā sthirā guhyā dēvikā chakrarūpiṇī ॥ 42 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Kashyapi (daughter of Kashyapa), Kamala-devi (lotus goddess), Nada-chakra-nivasinī (dwelling in the wheel of sound), Mridhadhara (clay-supported), Sthira (stable), Guhya (secret), Devika (little goddess), Chakrarupinī (discus-formed). (42)

contentBlocks.original:
अविद्या शार्वरी भुञ्जा जम्भासुरनिबर्हिणी ।
श्रीकाया श्रीकला शुभ्रा कर्मनिर्मूलकारिणी ॥ 43 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
avidyā śārvarī bhuñjā jambhāsuranibarhiṇī ।
śrīkāyā śrīkalā śubhrā karmanirmūlakāriṇī ॥ 43 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Avidya (ignorance itself), Sharvari (night), Bhunja (she who enjoys), Jambhasura-nibarhini (slayer of the demon Jambha), Shrikaya (auspicious-bodied), Shrikala (auspicious art), Shubhra (brilliant), Karma-nirmula-karini (uprooter of karma). (43)

contentBlocks.original:
आदिलक्ष्मीर्गुणाधारा पञ्चब्रह्मात्मिका परा ।
श्रुतिर्ब्रह्ममुखावासा सर्वसम्पत्तिरूपिणी ॥ 44 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
ādilakṣmīrguṇādhārā pañchabrahmātmikā parā ।
śrutirbrahmamukhāvāsā sarvasampattirūpiṇī ॥ 44 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Adi-lakshmi (primal Lakshmi), Gunadhara (foundation of qualities), Pancha-brahmatmika (essence of the five Brahmas), Para (supreme), Shruti (Vedic hearing), Brahma-mukha-vasa (dwelling on Brahma's face), Sarva-sampatti-rupinī (whose form is all prosperity). (44)

contentBlocks.original:
मृतसञ्जीवनी मैत्री कामिनी कामवर्जिता ।
निर्वाणमार्गदा देवी हंसिनी काशिका क्षमा ॥ 45 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
mṛtasañjīvanī maitrī kāminī kāmavarjitā ।
nirvāṇamārgadā dēvī haṃsinī kāśikā kṣamā ॥ 45 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Mrita-sanjivani (reviver of the dead), Maitri (friendliness), Kamini (the desirous), Kamavarjita (free of desire), Nirvana-margada (giver of the path to liberation), Devi (goddess), Hamsini (swan-formed), Kashika (the Kashi goddess), Kshama (patience). (45)

contentBlocks.original:
सपर्या गुणिनी भिन्ना निर्गुणा खण्डिताशुभा ।
स्वामिनी वेदिनी शक्या शाम्बरी चक्रधारिणी ॥ 46 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
saparyā guṇinī bhinnā nirguṇā khaṇḍitāśubhā ।
svāminī vēdinī śakyā śāmbarī chakradhāriṇī ॥ 46 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Saparya (worthy of worship), Gunini (endowed with virtues), Bhinna (distinct), Nirguna (beyond attributes), Khanditashubha (cutter of inauspiciousness), Svamini (mistress), Vedini (knower of the Vedas), Shakya (capable), Shambari (conjurer), Chakradharini (discus-bearer). (46)

contentBlocks.original:
दण्डिनी मुण्डिनी व्याघ्री शिखिनी सोमसंहतिः ।
चिन्तामणिश्चिदानन्दा पञ्चबाणप्रबोधिनी ॥ 47 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
daṇḍinī muṇḍinī vyāghrī śikhinī sōmasaṃhatiḥ ।
chintāmaṇiśchidānandā pañchabāṇaprabōdhinī ॥ 47 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Dandini (staff-bearer), Mundini (skull-bearer), Vyaghri (tigress), Shikhini (peacock-crested), Soma-samhati (mass of moonlight), Chintamani (wish-fulfilling gem), Chidananda (consciousness-bliss), Panchana-prabodhini (awakener of the five arrows of Kama). (47)

contentBlocks.original:
बाणश्रेणिस्सहस्राक्षी सहस्रभुजपादुका ।
सन्ध्यावलिस्त्रिसन्ध्याख्या ब्रह्माण्डमणिभूषणा ॥ 48 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
bāṇaśrēṇissahasrākṣī sahasrabhujapādukā ।
sandhyāvalistrisandhyākhyā brahmāṇḍamaṇibhūṣaṇā ॥ 48 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Banashreni (row of arrows), Sahasrakshi (thousand-eyed), Sahasrabhuja-paduka (with a thousand arms and feet), Sandhya-vali (twilight garland), Trisandhya-khya (called the three twilights), Brahmanda-mani-bhushana (jewel-ornament of the cosmic egg). (48)

contentBlocks.original:
वासवी वारुणीसेना कुलिका मन्त्ररञ्जनी ।
जितप्राणस्वरूपा च कान्ता काम्यवरप्रदा ॥ 49 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
vāsavī vāruṇīsēnā kuḻikā mantrarañjanī ।
jitaprāṇasvarūpā cha kāntā kāmyavarapradā ॥ 49 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Vasavi (Indra's goddess), Varunisenā (Varuna's army), Kulika (of noble lineage), Mantra-ranjani (mantra-delighter), Jita-prana-svarupa (whose form is the conquered vital breath), Kanta (beloved), Kamyavaraprada (giver of desired boons). (49)

contentBlocks.original:
मन्त्रब्राह्मणविद्यार्था नादरूपा हविष्मती ।
आथर्वणिः श्रुतिः शून्या कल्पनावर्जिता सती ॥ 50 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
mantrabrāhmaṇavidyārthā nādarūpā haviṣmatī ।
ātharvaṇiḥ śrutiḥ śūnyā kalpanāvarjitā satī ॥ 50 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Mantra-brahmavidyartha (object of mantra, Brahman, and knowledge), Nadarupa (sound-formed), Havishmati (holder of oblations), Atharvaṇi (of the Atharva Veda), Shruti (revelation), Shunya (the void), Kalpanavarjita (beyond imagination), Sati (the devoted wife). (50)

contentBlocks.original:
सत्ताजातिः प्रमाऽमेयाऽप्रमितिः प्राणदा गतिः ।
अवर्णा पञ्चवर्णा च सर्वदा भुवनेश्वरी ॥ 51 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
sattājātiḥ pramā'mēyā'pramitiḥ prāṇadā gatiḥ ।
avarṇā pañchavarṇā cha sarvadā bhuvanēśvarī ॥ 51 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Satta (pure being), Jati (category), Prama (valid knowledge), Ameya (immeasurable), Apramiti (beyond measure), Pranadā (life-giver), Gati (goal), Avarna (colourless), Panchavarṇā (five-coloured), Sarvada (always), Bhuvaneshvari (mistress of the worlds). (51)

contentBlocks.original:
त्रैलोक्यमोहिनी विद्या सर्वभर्त्री क्षराऽक्षरा ।
हिरण्यवर्णा हरिणी सर्वोपद्रवनाशिनी ॥ 52 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
trailōkyamōhinī vidyā sarvabhartrī kṣarā'kṣarā ।
hiraṇyavarṇā hariṇī sarvōpadravanāśinī ॥ 52 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Trailokya-mohini (enchantress of the three worlds), Vidya (knowledge), Sarvabhatri (supporter of all), Kshara (the perishable), Akshara (the imperishable), Hiranyavarna (golden-hued), Harini (doe), Sarvopadrava-nashinī (destroyer of all calamities). (52)

contentBlocks.original:
कैवल्यपदवीरेखा सूर्यमण्डलसंस्थिता ।
सोममण्डलमध्यस्था वह्निमण्डलसंस्थिता ॥ 53 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
kaivalyapadavīrēkhā sūryamaṇḍalasaṃsthitā ।
sōmamaṇḍalamadhyasthā vahnimaṇḍalasaṃsthitā ॥ 53 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Kaivalyapadavirekha (sign of the state of liberation), Surya-mandala-samsthita (dwelling in the solar orb), Soma-mandala-madhyastha (stationed in the midst of the lunar orb), Vahni-mandala-samsthita (dwelling in the orb of fire). (53)

contentBlocks.original:
वायुमण्डलमध्यस्था व्योममण्डलसंस्थिता ।
चक्रिका चक्रमध्यस्था चक्रमार्गप्रवर्तिनी ॥ 54 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
vāyumaṇḍalamadhyasthā vyōmamaṇḍalasaṃsthitā ।
chakrikā chakramadhyasthā chakramārgapravartinī ॥ 54 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Vayu-mandala-madhyastha (stationed in the midst of the wind orb), Vyoma-mandala-samsthita (dwelling in the ether orb), Chakrika (discus-wielder), Chakra-madhyastha (stationed in the centre of the wheel), Chakramarga-pravartini (she who sets the wheel-path in motion). (54)

contentBlocks.original:
कोकिलाकुलचक्रेशा पक्षतिः पङ्क्तिपावनी ।
सर्वसिद्धान्तमार्गस्था षड्वर्णावरवर्जिता ॥ 55 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
kōkilākulachakrēśā pakṣatiḥ paṅktipāvanī ।
sarvasiddhāntamārgasthā ṣaḍvarṇāvaravarjitā ॥ 55 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Kokilakulachakreshā (queen of the multitude of cuckoos), Pakshati (wing-spread), Pankti-pavani (purifier of the row), Sarvasiddhanata-margas-tha (standing on the path of all doctrines), Shadvarnaavaravarjita (free from the enclosure of six colours/letters). (55)

contentBlocks.original:
शररुद्रहरा हन्त्री सर्वसंहारकारिणी ।
पुरुषा पौरुषी तुष्टिस्सर्वतन्त्रप्रसूतिका ॥ 56 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
śararudraharā hantrī sarvasaṃhārakāriṇī ।
puruṣā pauruṣī tuṣṭissarvatantraprasūtikā ॥ 56 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Shararudra-hara (remover of the arrow of Rudra), Hantri (the slayer), Sarvasamhara-karini (agent of all dissolution), Purusha (the person/spirit), Paurushi (heroic), Tushti (contentment), Sarvatantra-prasutika (mother of all tantras). (56)

contentBlocks.original:
अर्धनारीश्वरी देवी सर्वविद्याप्रदायिनी ।
भार्गवी याजुषीविद्या सर्वोपनिषदास्थिता ॥ 57 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
ardhanārīśvarī dēvī sarvavidyāpradāyinī ।
bhārgavī yājuṣīvidyā sarvōpaniṣadāsthitā ॥ 57 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Ardha-narishvari (half-woman half-lord), Devi (goddess), Sarvavidya-pradayini (bestower of all knowledge), Bhargavi (daughter of Bhrigu/Lakshmi), Yajushi-vidya (Yajurvedic knowledge), Sarva-upanishadasthita (established in all Upanishads), Vyomakesha (sky-haired), Akhila-prana (all-pervading life), Panchakoshavilakshana (distinguished from the five sheaths), Panchakoshatmika (essence of the five sheaths), Pratyak (the inner self), Panchaborahmatmika (essence of the five Brahmas), Shiva (auspicious). (57–58)

contentBlocks.original:
व्योमकेशाखिलप्राणा पञ्चकोशविलक्षणा ।
पञ्चकोशात्मिका प्रत्यक्पञ्चब्रह्मात्मिका शिवा ॥ 58 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
vyōmakēśākhilaprāṇā pañchakōśavilakṣaṇā ।
pañchakōśātmikā pratyakpañchabrahmātmikā śivā ॥ 58 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Ardha-narishvari (half-woman half-lord), Devi (goddess), Sarvavidya-pradayini (bestower of all knowledge), Bhargavi (daughter of Bhrigu/Lakshmi), Yajushi-vidya (Yajurvedic knowledge), Sarva-upanishadasthita (established in all Upanishads), Vyomakesha (sky-haired), Akhila-prana (all-pervading life), Panchakoshavilakshana (distinguished from the five sheaths), Panchakoshatmika (essence of the five sheaths), Pratyak (the inner self), Panchaborahmatmika (essence of the five Brahmas), Shiva (auspicious). (57–58)

contentBlocks.original:
जगज्जराजनित्री च पञ्चकर्मप्रसूतिका ।
वाग्देव्याभरणाकारा सर्वकाम्यस्थितास्थितिः ॥ 59 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
jagajjarājanitrī cha pañchakarmaprasūtikā ।
vāgdēvyābharaṇākārā sarvakāmyasthitāsthitiḥ ॥ 59 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Jagajjara-janitri (mother of world-age), Panchakarmaprasutika (mother of the five actions), Vagdevya-bharanakara (adorned with the ornaments of the speech-goddess), Sarvakamyasthitasthiti (the abiding state of all desires). (59)

contentBlocks.original:
अष्टादशचतुष्षष्ठिपीठिका विद्यया युता ।
कालिकाकर्षणश्यामा यक्षिणी किन्नरेश्वरी ॥ 60 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
aṣṭādaśachatuṣṣaṣṭhipīṭhikā vidyayā yutā ।
kāḻikākarṣaṇaśyāmā yakṣiṇī kinnarēśvarī ॥ 60 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Ashtadasha-chatusshashti-pithika (enshrined in the eighteen and sixty-four sacred seats), Vidyaya-yuta (endowed with knowledge), Kalikakarshanashyama (dark as the pull of Kali), Yakshini (the yaksha spirit), Kinnare-shvari (mistress of the Kinnaras). (60)

contentBlocks.original:
केतकी मल्लिकाऽशोका वाराही धरणी ध्रुवा ।
नारसिंही महोग्रास्या भक्तानामार्तिनाशिनी ॥ 61 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
kētakī mallikā'śōkā vārāhī dharaṇī dhruvā ।
nārasiṃhī mahōgrāsyā bhaktānāmārtināśinī ॥ 61 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Ketaki (the ketaki flower), Mallika (jasmine), Ashoka (the Ashoka tree), Varahi (boar-headed goddess), Dharani (the earth), Dhruva (the pole star), Narasimhi (the lioness-Narasimha goddess), Mahograsyā (of fearsome great face), Bhaktanam-artinashinī (destroyer of devotees' suffering). (61)

contentBlocks.original:
अन्तर्बला स्थिरा लक्ष्मीर्जरामरणनाशिनी ।
श्रीरञ्जिता महाकाया सोमसूर्याग्निलोचना ॥ 62 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
antarbalā sthirā lakṣmīrjarāmaraṇanāśinī ।
śrīrañjitā mahākāyā sōmasūryāgnilōchanā ॥ 62 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Antarabala (inner strength), Sthira (steadfast), Lakshmi (prosperity), Jara-marana-nashinī (destroyer of old age and death), Shriranjita (delighted by glory), Mahakaya (great-bodied), Soma-surya-agni-lochana (with eyes of moon, sun, and fire). (62)

contentBlocks.original:
अदितिर्देवमाता च अष्टपुत्राऽष्टयोगिनी ।
अष्टप्रकृतिरष्टाष्टविभ्राजद्विकृताकृतिः ॥ 63 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
aditirdēvamātā cha aṣṭaputrā'ṣṭayōginī ।
aṣṭaprakṛtiraṣṭāṣṭavibhrājadvikṛtākṛtiḥ ॥ 63 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Aditi (the boundless), Deva-mata (mother of gods), Ashta-putra (mother of eight sons), Ashtayogini (eight yoginis), Ashtaprakriti (eight primal natures), Ashtaashta-vibhrajad-vikritakriti (splendidly manifold in eight-times-eight forms). (63)

contentBlocks.original:
दुर्भिक्षध्वंसिनी देवी सीता सत्या च रुक्मिणी ।
ख्यातिजा भार्गवी देवी देवयोनिस्तपस्विनी ॥ 64 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
durbhikṣadhvaṃsinī dēvī sītā satyā cha rukmiṇī ।
khyātijā bhārgavī dēvī dēvayōnistapasvinī ॥ 64 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Durbhikshadhvamshinī (destroyer of famine), Devi (goddess), Sita (Rama's consort), Satya (truth), Rukmini (Krishna's consort), Khyatija (born of fame), Bhargavi (Lakshmi, daughter of Bhrigu), Devi (goddess), Devayoni (divine womb), Tapasvinī (the ascetic). (64)

contentBlocks.original:
शाकम्भरी महाशोणा गरुडोपरिसंस्थिता ।
सिंहगा व्याघ्रगा देवी वायुगा च महाद्रिगा ॥ 65 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
śākambharī mahāśōṇā garuḍōparisaṃsthitā ।
siṃhagā vyāghragā dēvī vāyugā cha mahādrigā ॥ 65 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Shakambhari (feeder of vegetables), Mahashona (great red one), Garuda-upari-samsthita (mounted above Garuda), Simhaga (lion-riding), Vyaghragā (tiger-riding), Devi (goddess), Vayuga (wind-riding), Mahadriga (mountain-riding). (65)

contentBlocks.original:
अकारादिक्षकारान्ता सर्वविद्याधिदेवता ।
मन्त्रव्याख्याननिपुणा ज्योतिश्शास्त्रैकलोचना ॥ 66 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
akārādikṣakārāntā sarvavidyādhidēvatā ।
mantravyākhyānanipuṇā jyōtiśśāstraikalōchanā ॥ 66 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Akaradikshakaranta (from the letter A to Ksha), Sarvavidyadhidevata (presiding deity of all knowledge), Mantra-vyakhyana-nipuna (expert in mantra exposition), Jyotishsastra-ika-lochana (single eye of the science of astrology). (66)

contentBlocks.original:
इडापिङ्गलिकामध्यासुषुम्ना ग्रन्थिभेदिनी ।
कालचक्राश्रयोपेता कालचक्रस्वरूपिणी ॥ 67 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
iḍāpiṅgaḻikāmadhyāsuṣumnā granthibhēdinī ।
kālachakrāśrayōpētā kālachakrasvarūpiṇī ॥ 67 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Ida-Pingalika-madhya (between the Ida and Pingala), Sushumna-granthibhedini (piercer of the knots of Sushumna), Kalachakrashrayopeta (endowed with the support of the wheel of time), Kalachakra-svarupini (whose form is the wheel of time). (67)

contentBlocks.original:
वैशारदी मतिश्श्रेष्ठा वरिष्ठा सर्वदीपिका ।
वैनायकी वरारोहा श्रोणिवेला बहिर्वलिः ॥ 68 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
vaiśāradī matiśśrēṣṭhā variṣṭhā sarvadīpikā ।
vaināyakī varārōhā śrōṇivēlā bahirvaliḥ ॥ 68 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Vaisharadi (learned), Mati-shreshtha (best of intellects), Varishthas (most excellent), Sarva-dipika (light of all), Vinayaki (Ganeshini), Vararoha (of noble frame), Shronivela (the tide of the hips), Bahirvali (outer boundary). (68)

contentBlocks.original:
जम्भिनी जृम्भिणी जम्भकारिणी गणकारिका ।
शरणी चक्रिकाऽनन्ता सर्वव्याधिचिकित्सकी ॥ 69 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
jambhinī jṛmbhiṇī jambhakāriṇī gaṇakārikā ।
śaraṇī chakrikā'nantā sarvavyādhichikitsakī ॥ 69 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Jambhini (stupefier), Jrimbhini (yawner), Jambha-karini (performer of stupefaction), Ganakarika (doer of calculation), Sharani (the shelter), Chakrika (discus-bearer), Ananta (boundless), Sarvavyadhi-chikitsaki (healer of all diseases). (69)

contentBlocks.original:
देवकी देवसङ्काशा वारिधिः करुणाकरा ।
शर्वरी सर्वसम्पन्ना सर्वपापप्रभञ्जनी ॥ 70 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
dēvakī dēvasaṅkāśā vāridhiḥ karuṇākarā ।
śarvarī sarvasampannā sarvapāpaprabhañjanī ॥ 70 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Devaki (divine), Deva-sankasha (radiant as gods), Varidhi (the ocean), Karunakaras (ocean of compassion), Sharvari (the night), Sarvasampanna (endowed with all), Sarvapapa-prabhanjani (destroyer of all sins). (70)

contentBlocks.original:
एकमात्रा द्विमात्रा च त्रिमात्रा च तथाऽपरा ।
अर्धमात्रा परा सूक्ष्मा सूक्ष्मार्थाऽर्थपराऽपरा ॥ 71 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
ēkamātrā dvimātrā cha trimātrā cha tathā'parā ।
ardhamātrā parā sūkṣmā sūkṣmārthā'rthaparā'parā ॥ 71 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Ekamatra (single syllable), Dvimatra (two syllables), Trimatra (three syllables), and also Apara (another, beyond), Ardhamatra (half-syllable), Para (supreme), Sukshma (subtle), Sukshmarthā (subtly meaningful), Arthapara (para in meaning), Apara (beyond). (71)

contentBlocks.original:
एकवीरा विशेषाख्या षष्ठीदेवी मनस्विनी ।
नैष्कर्म्या निष्कलालोका ज्ञानकर्माधिका गुणा ॥ 72 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
ēkavīrā viśēṣākhyā ṣaṣṭhīdēvī manasvinī ।
naiṣkarmyā niṣkalālōkā jñānakarmādhikā guṇā ॥ 72 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Ekavira (sole hero), Visheshakhya (specially named), Shashthi-devi (the sixth-day goddess), Manasvinī (the thoughtful one), Naishkarmya (non-doership), Nishkala-loka (world of the partless), Jñanakarmādhika (superior through knowledge and action), Guna (quality). (72)

contentBlocks.original:
सबन्ध्वानन्दसन्दोहा व्योमाकाराऽनिरूपिता ।
गद्यपद्यात्मिका वाणी सर्वालङ्कारसंयुता ॥ 73 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
sabandhvānandasandōhā vyōmākārā'nirūpitā ।
gadyapadyātmikā vāṇī sarvālaṅkārasaṃyutā ॥ 73 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Sabandhvananda-sandoha (mass of the bliss of kinship), Vyomakara (sky-formed), Anirupita (undescribed), Gadyapadyatmika (whose form is prose and verse), Vani (speech), Sarvalankara-samyuta (adorned with all ornaments of rhetoric). (73)

contentBlocks.original:
साधुबन्धपदन्यासा सर्वौको घटिकावलिः ।
षट्कर्मा कर्कशाकारा सर्वकर्मविवर्जिता ॥ 74 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
sādhubandhapadanyāsā sarvaukō ghaṭikāvaliḥ ।
ṣaṭkarmā karkaśākārā sarvakarmavivarjitā ॥ 74 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Sadhu-bandha-padanyasa (placing steps like a good bond), Sarvauko-ghatika-vali (string of all the timekeeper's jars), Shatkarmā (of six sacred duties), Karkasha-kara (hard-actioned), Sarvakarma-vivarjita (free from all karma). (74)

contentBlocks.original:
आदित्यवर्णा चापर्णा कामिनी वररूपिणी ।
ब्रह्माणी ब्रह्मसन्ताना वेदवागीश्वरी शिवा ॥ 75 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
ādityavarṇā chāparṇā kāminī vararūpiṇī ।
brahmāṇī brahmasantānā vēdavāgīśvarī śivā ॥ 75 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Adityavarna (sun-coloured), Chaparna (leaf-bodied), Kamini (desirous), Vara-rupini (boon-formed), Brahmani (Brahma's consort), Brahma-santana (Brahma's progeny), Vedavagishvari (Vedic sovereign of speech), Shiva (auspicious). (75)

contentBlocks.original:
पुराणन्यायमीमांसाधर्मशास्त्रागमश्रुता ।
सद्योवेदवती सर्वा हंसी विद्याधिदेवता ॥ 76 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
purāṇanyāyamīmāṃsādharmaśāstrāgamaśrutā ।
sadyōvēdavatī sarvā haṃsī vidyādhidēvatā ॥ 76 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Purana, Nyaya, Mimamsa, Dharmashastra, Agama, and Shruta (all heard) — she is knowledgeable in all. Sadyo-vedavati (immediately Vedic), Sarva (all), Hamsī (swan-formed), Vidyadhidevata (presiding deity of knowledge). (76)

contentBlocks.original:
विश्वेश्वरी जगद्धात्री विश्वनिर्माणकारिणी ।
वैदिकी वेदरूपा च कालिका कालरूपिणी ॥ 77 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
viśvēśvarī jagaddhātrī viśvanirmāṇakāriṇī ।
vaidikī vēdarūpā cha kālikā kālarūpiṇī ॥ 77 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Vishveshvari (sovereign of the universe), Jagadddhatri (sustainer of the world), Vishvanirmana-karini (agent of world-creation), Vaidiki (Vedic), Vedarupa (Veda-formed), Kalika (the dark one), Kalarupinī (time-formed). (77)

contentBlocks.original:
नारायणी महादेवी सर्वतत्त्वप्रवर्तिनी ।
हिरण्यवर्णरूपा च हिरण्यपदसम्भवा ॥ 78 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
nārāyaṇī mahādēvī sarvatattvapravartinī ।
hiraṇyavarṇarūpā cha hiraṇyapadasambhavā ॥ 78 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Narayani (Narayana's power), Mahadevi (great goddess), Sarva-tattva-pravartini (she who sets all principles in motion), Hiranya-varna-rupa (of golden-hued form), Hiranyapada-sambhava (born of golden feet). (78)

contentBlocks.original:
कैवल्यपदवी पुण्या कैवल्यज्ञानलक्षिता ।
ब्रह्मसम्पत्तिरूपा च ब्रह्मसम्पत्तिकारिणी ॥ 79 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
kaivalyapadavī puṇyā kaivalyajñānalakṣitā ।
brahmasampattirūpā cha brahmasampattikāriṇī ॥ 79 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Kaivalyapadavi (path of liberation), Punya (meritorious), Kaivalyajñana-lakshita (marked by the knowledge of liberation), Brahma-sampatti-rupa (form of Brahmic wealth), Brahma-sampatti-karini (bringer of Brahmic wealth). (79)

contentBlocks.original:
वारुणी वारुणाराध्या सर्वकर्मप्रवर्तिनी ।
एकाक्षरपराऽऽयुक्ता सर्वदारिद्र्यभञ्जिनी ॥ 80 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
vāruṇī vāruṇārādhyā sarvakarmapravartinī ।
ēkākṣaraparā''yuktā sarvadāridryabhañjinī ॥ 80 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Varuni (Varuna's goddess), Varunāradhya (worshipped by Varuna), Sarvakarma-pravartini (she who sets all actions in motion), Ekakshara-para (supreme single-syllable), Ayukta (connected), Sarvadaridrya-bhanjini (destroyer of all poverty). (80)

contentBlocks.original:
पाशाङ्कुशान्विता दिव्या वीणाव्याख्याक्षसूत्रभृत् ।
एकमूर्तिस्त्रयीमूर्तिर्मधुकैटभभञ्जिनी ॥ 81 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
pāśāṅkuśānvitā divyā vīṇāvyākhyākṣasūtrabhṛt ।
ēkamūrtistrayīmūrtirmadhukaiṭabhabhañjinī ॥ 81 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Pashanku-shanvita (with noose and goad), Divya (divine), Vina-vyakhya-akshasutrabrit (holding veena, commentary, and rosary), Ekamurtis (single-formed), Trayimurtis (triple-formed), Madhu-Kaitabha-bhanjini (destroyer of Madhu and Kaitabha). (81)

contentBlocks.original:
साङ्ख्या साङ्ख्यवती ज्वाला ज्वलन्ती कामरूपिणी ।
जाग्रती सर्वसम्पत्तिस्सुषुप्ता स्वेष्टदायिनी ॥ 82 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
sāṅkhyā sāṅkhyavatī jvālā jvalantī kāmarūpiṇī ।
jāgratī sarvasampattissuṣuptā svēṣṭadāyinī ॥ 82 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Sankhyā (Samkhya philosophy), Sankhyavati (endowed with Samkhya), Jvala (flame), Jvalanti (blazing), Kama-rupini (desire-formed), Jagrati (the waking), Sarva-sampatti (all-prosperity), Sushupti (deep sleep), Sveshta-dayini (giver of one's desired). (82)

contentBlocks.original:
कपालिनी महादंष्ट्रा भ्रुकुटी कुटिलानना ।
सर्वावासा सुवासा च बृहत्यष्टिश्च शक्वरी ॥ 83 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
kapālinī mahādaṃṣṭrā bhrukuṭī kuṭilānanā ।
sarvāvāsā suvāsā cha bṛhatyaṣṭiścha śakvarī ॥ 83 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Kapalini (skull-bearer), Mahadamshtra (great-fanged), Bhrukuti (frowning), Kutilānana (crooked-faced), Sarvavāsa (dwelling everywhere), Suvasa (fragrant-dwelling), Brihati (the vast), Ashti (existence), Shakvari (the powerful). (83)

contentBlocks.original:
छन्दोगणप्रतिष्ठा च कल्माषी करुणात्मिका ।
चक्षुष्मती महाघोषा खड्गचर्मधराऽशनिः ॥ 84 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
Chandōgaṇapratiṣṭhā cha kalmāṣī karuṇātmikā ।
chakṣuṣmatī mahāghōṣā khaḍgacharmadharā'śaniḥ ॥ 84 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Chandogana-pratishtha (established in the chhandas groups), Kalmashi (the spotted one), Karunatmika (compassionate in nature), Chakshushmati (gifted with sight), Mahaghosha (great-voiced), Khadga-charma-dhara (sword and shield bearer), Ashani (thunderbolt). (84)

contentBlocks.original:
शिल्पवैचित्र्यविद्योता सर्वतोभद्रवासिनी ।
अचिन्त्यलक्षणाकारा सूत्रभाष्यनिबन्धना ॥ 85 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
śilpavaichitryavidyōtā sarvatōbhadravāsinī ।
achintyalakṣaṇākārā sūtrabhāṣyanibandhanā ॥ 85 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Shilpa-vaichitryavidyota (illuminating the wonder of crafts), Sarvatobhadra-vasini (dwelling in the auspicious everywhere), Achintya-lakshana-kara (of inconceivable features), Sutra-bhashya-nibandhana (binding sutra, commentary, and treatise). (85)

contentBlocks.original:
सर्ववेदार्थसम्पत्तिस्सर्वशास्त्रार्थमातृका ।
अकारादिक्षकारान्तसर्ववर्णकृतस्थला ॥ 86 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
sarvavēdārthasampattissarvaśāstrārthamātṛkā ।
akārādikṣakārāntasarvavarṇakṛtasthalā ॥ 86 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Sarvavedartha-sampatti (wealth of the meaning of all Vedas), Sarvashastrartha-matrika (mother of the meaning of all scriptures), Akaradikshakaranta-sarvavarnakritas-thala (comprising all letters from A to Ksha). (86)

contentBlocks.original:
सर्वलक्ष्मीस्सदानन्दा सारविद्या सदाशिवा ।
सर्वज्ञा सर्वशक्तिश्च खेचरीरूपगोच्छ्रिता ॥ 87 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
sarvalakṣmīssadānandā sāravidyā sadāśivā ।
sarvajñā sarvaśaktiścha khēcharīrūpagōchChritā ॥ 87 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Sarvalakshmi (all-Lakshmi), Sadananda (eternal bliss), Saravidya (essence-knowledge), Sadashiva (eternal Shiva), Sarvajna (omniscient), Sarvashakti (omnipotent), Khechari-rupa-gochhrita (assumed the space-roaming form). (87)

contentBlocks.original:
अणिमादिगुणोपेता परा काष्ठा परा गतिः ।
हंसयुक्तविमानस्था हंसारूढा शशिप्रभा ॥ 88 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
aṇimādiguṇōpētā parā kāṣṭhā parā gatiḥ ।
haṃsayuktavimānasthā haṃsārūḍhā śaśiprabhā ॥ 88 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Animadiguna-upeta (endowed with the eight powers beginning with Anima), Para (supreme), Kashtha (the extreme limit), Para-gati (supreme destination), Hamsa-yukta-vimanas-tha (seated in a swan-drawn aerial car), Hamsarudha (swan-mounted), Shashiprabhā (moon-radiant). (88)

contentBlocks.original:
भवानी वासनाशक्तिराकृतिस्थाखिलाऽखिला ।
तन्त्रहेतुर्विचित्राङ्गी व्योमगङ्गाविनोदिनी ॥ 89 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
bhavānī vāsanāśaktirākṛtisthākhilā'khilā ।
tantrahēturvichitrāṅgī vyōmagaṅgāvinōdinī ॥ 89 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Bhavani (world-goddess), Vasana-shakti (power of latent impressions), Akriti-stha (dwelling in form), Akhila (the all), Tantra-hetu (cause of Tantra), Vichitrangi (of wondrous limbs), Vyoma-ganga-vinodini (sporting in the heavenly Ganga). (89)

contentBlocks.original:
वर्षा च वार्षिका चैव ऋग्यजुस्सामरूपिणी ।
महानदीनदीपुण्याऽगण्यपुण्यगुणक्रिया ॥ 90 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
varṣā cha vārṣikā chaiva ṛgyajussāmarūpiṇī ।
mahānadīnadīpuṇyā'gaṇyapuṇyaguṇakriyā ॥ 90 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Varsha (rain), Varshika (the rainy season), Rig-Yajus-Sama-rupini (formed of the Rig, Yajur, and Sama Vedas), Mahanadi (great river), Nadi-punya (holy river), Agannya-punya-guna-kriya (of incalculable meritorious virtues and actions). (90)

contentBlocks.original:
समाधिगतलभ्यार्था श्रोतव्या स्वप्रिया घृणा ।
नामाक्षरपरा देवी उपसर्गनखाञ्चिता ॥ 91 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
samādhigatalabhyārthā śrōtavyā svapriyā ghṛṇā ।
nāmākṣaraparā dēvī upasarganakhāñchitā ॥ 91 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Samadhigata-labhyartha (the object attained through samadhi), Shrotavya (worthy of hearing), Svapriya (self-beloved), Ghrina (compassion), Namakshara-para (supreme in letters of name), Devi (goddess), Upasarganakhanchita (with claws adorned with verbal prefixes). (91)

contentBlocks.original:
निपातोरुद्वयीजङ्घा मातृका मन्त्ररूपिणी ।
आसीना च शयाना च तिष्ठन्ती धावनाधिका ॥ 92 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
nipātōrudvayījaṅghā mātṛkā mantrarūpiṇī ।
āsīnā cha śayānā cha tiṣṭhantī dhāvanādhikā ॥ 92 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Nipatoru-dvayijangha (with thighs like upward particles and calves like dual forms), Matrika (the mother-goddess of letters), Mantra-rupini (mantra-formed), Asina (seated), Shayana (reclining), Tishtthanti (standing), Dhavana-dhika (excelling in running). (92)

contentBlocks.original:
लक्ष्यलक्षणयोगाढ्या ताद्रूप्यगणनाकृतिः ।
सैकरूपा नैकरूपा सेन्दुरूपा तदाकृतिः ॥ 93 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
lakṣyalakṣaṇayōgāḍhyā tādrūpyagaṇanākṛtiḥ ।
saikarūpā naikarūpā sēndurūpā tadākṛtiḥ ॥ 93 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Lakshya-lakshana-yogadhya (rich in the union of sign and signified), Tadrupya-gananakrti (form of the count of likeness), Saika-rupa (with one form), Naika-rupa (with many forms), Sendurupa (with the form of Indra), Tadakriti (of that form). (93)

contentBlocks.original:
समासतद्धिताकारा विभक्तिवचनात्मिका ।
स्वाहाकारा स्वधाकारा श्रीपत्यर्धाङ्गनन्दिनी ॥ 94 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
samāsataddhitākārā vibhaktivachanātmikā ।
svāhākārā svadhākārā śrīpatyardhāṅganandinī ॥ 94 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Samasa-taddhitakara (of the form of compound and secondary suffixes), Vibhakti-vachanatmika (of the nature of case-endings and number), Svahakara (of the form of svaha), Svadhakara (of the form of svadha), Shripatya-rdhanganandinī (delighting in the half-body of Shripati/Vishnu). (94)

contentBlocks.original:
गम्भीरा गहना गुह्या योनिलिङ्गार्धधारिणी ।
शेषवासुकिसंसेव्या चपला वरवर्णिनी ॥ 95 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
gambhīrā gahanā guhyā yōniliṅgārdhadhāriṇī ।
śēṣavāsukisaṃsēvyā chapalā varavarṇinī ॥ 95 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Gambhira (profound), Gahana (dense), Guhya (secret), Yoni-lingardha-dharini (bearer of the half-mark of yoni and linga), Shesha-vasuki-samsevya (served by Shesha and Vasuki), Chapala (quick), Varavarnini (of excellent complexion). (95)

contentBlocks.original:
कारुण्याकारसम्पत्तिः कीलकृन्मन्त्रकीलिका ।
शक्तिबीजात्मिका सर्वमन्त्रेष्टाक्षयकामना ॥ 96 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
kāruṇyākārasampattiḥ kīlakṛnmantrakīlikā ।
śaktibījātmikā sarvamantrēṣṭākṣayakāmanā ॥ 96 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Karunyakara-sampatti (wealth of compassionate form), Kilakrin-mantra-kilika (mantra-pin that pins all), Shakti-bijtmika (essence of the power-seed), Sarvamantra-ishta-kshaya-kamana (desiring the inexhaustible wishes of all mantras). (96)

contentBlocks.original:
आग्नेयी पार्थिवा आप्या वायव्या व्योमकेतना ।
सत्यज्ञानात्मिकाऽऽनन्दा ब्राह्मी ब्रह्म सनातनी ॥ 97 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
āgnēyī pārthivā āpyā vāyavyā vyōmakētanā ।
satyajñānātmikā''nandā brāhmī brahma sanātanī ॥ 97 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Agneyi (of fire), Parthiva (of earth), Apya (of water), Vayavya (of wind), Vyoma-ketana (sky-bannered), Satya-jñanatmika (of the nature of truth and knowledge), Ananda (bliss), Brahmi (Brahma's power), Brahma-sanatani (eternal Brahman). (97)

contentBlocks.original:
अविद्यावासना मायाप्रकृतिस्सर्वमोहिनी ।
शक्तिर्धारणशक्तिश्च चिदचिच्छक्तियोगिनी ॥ 98 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
avidyāvāsanā māyāprakṛtissarvamōhinī ।
śaktirdhāraṇaśaktiścha chidachichChaktiyōginī ॥ 98 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Avidya (ignorance), Vasana (latent impressions), Maya (illusion), Prakriti (primal nature), Sarvamohini (enchantress of all), Shakti (power), Dharana-shakti (holding power), Chidachichakti-yogini (yogini of conscious and unconscious powers). (98)

contentBlocks.original:
वक्त्रारुणा महामाया मरीचिर्मदमर्दिनी ।
विराट् स्वाहा स्वधा शुद्धा नीरूपास्तिस्सुभक्तिगा ॥ 99 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
vaktrāruṇā mahāmāyā marīchirmadamardinī ।
virāṭ svāhā svadhā śuddhā nīrūpāstissubhaktigā ॥ 99 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Vaktra-runa (ruddy-faced), Mahamaya (great illusion), Marichi (ray of light), Mada-mardini (crusher of arrogance), Virat (the cosmic form), Svaha (sacred offering), Svadha (ancestral offering), Shuddha (pure), Nirupa (without form), Asti (existence), Subhaktig (devotion-radiant). (99)

contentBlocks.original:
निरूपिताद्वयीविद्या नित्यानित्यस्वरूपिणी ।
वैराजमार्गसञ्चारा सर्वसत्पथदर्शिनी ॥ 100 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
nirūpitādvayīvidyā nityānityasvarūpiṇī ।
vairājamārgasañchārā sarvasatpathadarśinī ॥ 100 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Nirupita-dvayividya (the knowledge of the pair thus defined), Nityanitya-svarupini (whose form is both eternal and non-eternal), Vairaja-marga-sanchara (travelling the path of Viraja), Sarvasat-patha-darshinī (shower of all true paths). (100)

contentBlocks.original:
जालन्धरी मृडानी च भवानी भवभञ्जनी ।
त्रैकालिकज्ञानतन्तुस्त्रिकालज्ञानदायिनी ॥ 101 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
jālandharī mṛḍānī cha bhavānī bhavabhañjanī ।
traikālikajñānatantustrikālajñānadāyinī ॥ 101 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Jalandhara (the jalandhara goddess), Mridani (gentle one), Bhavani (world-goddess), Bhava-bhanjani (destroyer of existence), Traikalika-jñana-tantu (thread of knowledge of all three times), Trikala-jñana-dayini (giver of knowledge of past, present, and future). (101)

contentBlocks.original:
नादातीता स्मृतिः प्रज्ञा धात्रीरूपा त्रिपुष्करा ।
पराजिताविधानज्ञा विशेषितगुणात्मिका ॥ 102 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
nādātītā smṛtiḥ prajñā dhātrīrūpā tripuṣkarā ।
parājitāvidhānajñā viśēṣitaguṇātmikā ॥ 102 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Nadatita (beyond sound), Smriti (memory), Prajna (wisdom), Dhatri-rupa (nurturer-formed), Tripushkara (three-lotus), Parajita-vidhana-jña (knower of the method of the undefeated), Visheshita-gunatmika (whose nature is distinguished qualities). (102)

contentBlocks.original:
हिरण्यकेशिनी हेमब्रह्मसूत्रविचक्षणा ।
असङ्ख्येयपरार्धान्तस्वरव्यञ्जनवैखरी ॥ 103 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
hiraṇyakēśinī hēmabrahmasūtravichakṣaṇā ।
asaṅkhyēyaparārdhāntasvaravyañjanavaikharī ॥ 103 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Hiranyakeshini (golden-haired), Hema-brahmasutra-vichakshana (expert in golden sacred thread and Brahma-sutra), Asankhyeya-parardha-anta-svara-vyanjana-vaikhari (vaikhari speech of vowels and consonants unto countless highest numbers). (103)

contentBlocks.original:
मधुजिह्वा मधुमती मधुमासोदया मधुः ।
माधवी च महाभागा मेघगम्भीरनिस्वना ॥ 104 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
madhujihvā madhumatī madhumāsōdayā madhuḥ ।
mādhavī cha mahābhāgā mēghagambhīranisvanā ॥ 104 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Madhu-jihva (honey-tongued), Madhumati (honey-minded), Madhu-masodaya (arising in the honey month), Madhu (the sweet), Madhavi (spring-born), Mahabhaga (greatly fortunate), Megha-gambhira-nisvana (of a voice deep as thunder-clouds). (104)

contentBlocks.original:
ब्रह्मविष्णुमहेशादिज्ञातव्यार्थविशेषगा ।
नाभौ वह्निशिखाकारा ललाटे चन्द्रसन्निभा ॥ 105 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
brahmaviṣṇumahēśādijñātavyārthaviśēṣagā ।
nābhau vahniśikhākārā lalāṭē chandrasannibhā ॥ 105 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Brahma-Vishnu-Mahesha-adi-jñatavyartha-visheshaga (known as the special meaning of Brahma, Vishnu, Maheshvara and others), Nabhau-vahni-shikha-kara (fire-flame-formed at the navel), Lalate-chandra-sannibha (like the moon at the forehead). (105)

contentBlocks.original:
भ्रूमध्ये भास्कराकारा सर्वताराकृतिर्हृदि ।
कृत्तिकादिभरण्यन्तनक्षत्रेष्ट्यार्चितोदया ॥ 106 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
bhrūmadhyē bhāskarākārā sarvatārākṛtirhṛdi ।
kṛttikādibharaṇyantanakṣatrēṣṭyārchitōdayā ॥ 106 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Bhrumadhye-bhaskara-kara (sun-formed between the eyebrows), Sarva-tara-kriti-hridi (formed as all stars in the heart), Krittikadi-bharanyanta-nakshatra-ishtyarchitodaya (risen and worshipped from Krittika to Bharani among the stars). (106)

contentBlocks.original:
ग्रहविद्यात्मिका ज्योतिर्ज्योतिर्विन्मतिजीविका ।
ब्रह्माण्डगर्भिणी बाला सप्तावरणदेवता ॥ 107 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
grahavidyātmikā jyōtirjyōtirvinmatijīvikā ।
brahmāṇḍagarbhiṇī bālā saptāvaraṇadēvatā ॥ 107 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Grahava-idyatmika (essence of planetary knowledge), Jyoti (light), Jyoti-rinmati-jivika (whose life is a mind full of light), Brahmanda-garbhini (pregnant with the cosmic egg), Bala (the child-goddess), Sapta-varana-devata (presiding deity of the seven enclosures). (107)

contentBlocks.original:
वैराजोत्तमसाम्राज्या कुमारकुशलोदया ।
बगला भ्रमराम्बा च शिवदूती शिवात्मिका ॥ 108 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
vairājōttamasāmrājyā kumārakuśalōdayā ।
bagaḻā bhramarāmbā cha śivadūtī śivātmikā ॥ 108 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Virajo-ttama-samrajya (of the supreme sovereignty of Viraja), Kumara-kushalodaya (arising in the welfare of the young), Bagala (the crane-goddess), Bhramaramba (the bee-goddess), Shivaduti (Shiva's messenger), Shivatmika (Shiva-natured). (108)

contentBlocks.original:
मेरुविन्ध्यादिसंस्थाना काश्मीरपुरवासिनी ।
योगनिद्रा महानिद्रा विनिद्रा राक्षसाश्रिता ॥ 109 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
mēruvindhyādisaṃsthānā kāśmīrapuravāsinī ।
yōganidrā mahānidrā vinidrā rākṣasāśritā ॥ 109 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Meru-Vindhyadi-samsthana (dwelling in Meru, Vindhya, and other mountains), Kashmir-pura-vasini (dwelling in the city of Kashmir), Yoganidra (the sleep of yoga), Mahanidra (great sleep), Vinidra (sleepless), Rakshasashrita (sought by the demons). (109)

contentBlocks.original:
सुवर्णदा महागङ्गा पञ्चाख्या पञ्चसंहतिः ।
सुप्रजाता सुवीरा च सुपोषा सुपतिश्शिवा ॥ 110 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
suvarṇadā mahāgaṅgā pañchākhyā pañchasaṃhatiḥ ।
suprajātā suvīrā cha supōṣā supatiśśivā ॥ 110 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Suvarnada (giver of gold), Mahaganga (the great Ganga), Panchakhya (fivefold-named), Pancha-samhati (fivefold congregation), Suprajata (well-born), Suvira (heroic), Suposha (well-nourished), Supati (of a good husband), Shiva (auspicious). (110)

contentBlocks.original:
सुगृहा रक्तबीजान्ता हतकन्दर्पजीविका ।
समुद्रव्योममध्यस्था समबिन्दुसमाश्रया ॥ 111 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
sugṛhā raktabījāntā hatakandarpajīvikā ।
samudravyōmamadhyasthā samabindusamāśrayā ॥ 111 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Sugrha (of a good home), Rakta-bijah-anta (destroyer of Raktabija), Hata-kandarpa-jivika (she whose life is the slain Kama), Samudra-vyoma-madhyastha (stationed in the midst of ocean and sky), Sama-bindu-samashraya (equal refuge of bindu). (111)

contentBlocks.original:
सौभाग्यरसजीवातुस्सारासारविवेकदृक् ।
त्रिवल्यादिसुपुष्टाङ्गा भारती भरताश्रिता ॥ 112 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
saubhāgyarasajīvātussārāsāravivēkadṛk ।
trivalyādisupuṣṭāṅgā bhāratī bharatāśritā ॥ 112 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Saubhagya-rasa-jivathu (life-essence of the rasa of fortune), Sara-sara-vivekadrik (discerning eye of essence and non-essence), Trivalyadi-supushhthangi (with body well-nurtured from the triple fold), Bharati (speech-goddess), Bharatashrita (refuge of the land of Bharata). (112)

contentBlocks.original:
नादब्रह्ममयीविद्या ज्ञानब्रह्ममयीपरा ।
ब्रह्मनाडी निरुक्तिश्च ब्रह्मकैवल्यसाधनम् ॥ 113 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
nādabrahmamayīvidyā jñānabrahmamayīparā ।
brahmanāḍī niruktiścha brahmakaivalyasādhanam ॥ 113 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Nada-brahmamayividya (knowledge full of Nada-Brahman), Jñana-brahmamayipara (supreme, full of knowledge-Brahman), Brahma-nadi (Brahma's nadi), Nirukti (etymological explanation), Brahma-kaivalya-sadhana (means to Brahmic liberation). (113)

contentBlocks.original:
कालिकेयमहोदारवीर्यविक्रमरूपिणी ।
वडवाग्निशिखावक्त्रा महाकवलतर्पणा ॥ 114 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
kālikēyamahōdāravīryavikramarūpiṇī ।
vaḍavāgniśikhāvaktrā mahākavalatarpaṇā ॥ 114 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Kalikeyamahod-ara-virya-vikrama-rupini (formed with the vast heroism and valour of Kalikeya), Vadava-agni-shikhavaktra (with a face like the submarine fire's flame), Maha-kavala-tarpana (satisfied by great mouthfuls). (114)

contentBlocks.original:
महाभूता महादर्पा महासारा महाक्रतुः ।
पञ्जभूतमहाग्रासा पञ्चभूताधिदेवता ॥ 115 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
mahābhūtā mahādarpā mahāsārā mahākratuḥ ।
pañjabhūtamahāgrāsā pañchabhūtādhidēvatā ॥ 115 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Maha-bhuta (great being), Maha-darpa (greatly proud), Maha-sara (of great essence), Maha-kratu (of great sacrifice), Pancha-bhuta-mahagrasa (great devourer of the five elements), Pancha-bhuta-adhidevata (presiding deity of the five elements). (115)

contentBlocks.original:
सर्वप्रमाणा सम्पत्तिस्सर्वरोगप्रतिक्रिया ।
ब्रह्माण्डान्तर्बहिर्व्याप्ता विष्णुवक्षोविभूषिणी ॥ 116 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
sarvapramāṇā sampattissarvarōgapratikriyā ।
brahmāṇḍāntarbahirvyāptā viṣṇuvakṣōvibhūṣiṇī ॥ 116 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Sarva-pramana (all-valid-knowledge), Sampatti (prosperity), Sarvaroga-pratikriya (remedy for all diseases), Brahmanda-antar-bahir-vyapta (pervading within and without the cosmic egg), Vishnu-vaksho-vibhushini (adornment of Vishnu's chest). (116)

contentBlocks.original:
शाङ्करी विधिवक्त्रस्था प्रवरा वरहेतुकी ।
हेममाला शिखामाला त्रिशिखा पञ्चलोचना ॥ 117 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
śāṅkarī vidhivaktrasthā pravarā varahētukī ।
hēmamālā śikhāmālā triśikhā pañchalōchanā ॥ 117 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Shankari (Shankara's consort), Vidhi-vaktrastha (dwelling on the face of Brahma), Pravara (most excellent), Vara-hetuki (cause of boons), Hemamala (golden-garlanded), Shikhamala (crested-garlanded), Trishikha (triple-crested), Panchalochana (five-eyed). (117) Sarvagama-sadachara-maryada (measure of good conduct in all Agamas), Yatu-bhanjani (destroyer of black magic), Punyashloka-prabandhadhya (rich in collections of auspicious verses), Sarvantaryami-rupini (whose form is the inner ruler of all). (118)

contentBlocks.original:
सर्वागमसदाचारमर्यादा यातुभञ्जनी ।
पुण्यश्लोकप्रबन्धाढ्या सर्वान्तर्यामिरूपिणी ॥ 118 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
sarvāgamasadāchāramaryādā yātubhañjanī ।
puṇyaślōkaprabandhāḍhyā sarvāntaryāmirūpiṇī ॥ 118 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Shankari (Shankara's consort), Vidhi-vaktrastha (dwelling on the face of Brahma), Pravara (most excellent), Vara-hetuki (cause of boons), Hemamala (golden-garlanded), Shikhamala (crested-garlanded), Trishikha (triple-crested), Panchalochana (five-eyed). (117) Sarvagama-sadachara-maryada (measure of good conduct in all Agamas), Yatu-bhanjani (destroyer of black magic), Punyashloka-prabandhadhya (rich in collections of auspicious verses), Sarvantaryami-rupini (whose form is the inner ruler of all). (118)

contentBlocks.original:
सामगानसमाराध्या श्रोत्रकर्णरसायनम् ।
जीवलोकैकजीवातुर्भद्रोदारविलोकना ॥ 119 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
sāmagānasamārādhyā śrōtrakarṇarasāyanam ।
jīvalōkaikajīvāturbhadrōdāravilōkanā ॥ 119 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Samagana-samaradhya (worshipped through the singing of Sama Veda), Shrotra-karna-rasayana (nectar to the ears and hearing), Jivaloka-ika-jivathu (sole life-breath of the world of living beings), Bhadrodara-vilochana (with auspicious, generous gaze). (119)

contentBlocks.original:
तटित्कोटिलसत्कान्तिस्तरुणी हरिसुन्दरी ।
मीननेत्रा च सेन्द्राक्षी विशालाक्षी सुमङ्गला ॥ 120 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
taṭitkōṭilasatkāntistaruṇī harisundarī ।
mīnanētrā cha sēndrākṣī viśālākṣī sumaṅgaḻā ॥ 120 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Tatit-koti-lasat-kanti (brilliance of millions of lightning flashes), Taruni (the young one), Hari-sundari (Hari's beauty), Mina-netra (fish-eyed), Sendrakshi (with Indra's eyes), Vishalakshi (wide-eyed), Sumangala (greatly auspicious). (120)

contentBlocks.original:
सर्वमङ्गलसम्पन्ना साक्षान्मङ्गलदेवता ।
देहहृद्दीपिका दीप्तिर्जिह्वपापप्रणाशिनी ॥ 121 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
sarvamaṅgaḻasampannā sākṣānmaṅgaḻadēvatā ।
dēhahṛddīpikā dīptirjihvapāpapraṇāśinī ॥ 121 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Sarva-mangala-sampanna (endowed with all auspiciousness), Sakshan-mangala-devata (directly the auspicious deity), Deha-hrit-dipika (lamp of body and heart), Dipti (radiance), Jihva-papa-pranashini (destroyer of the sins of the tongue). (121)

contentBlocks.original:
अर्धचन्द्रोल्लसद्दंष्ट्रा यज्ञवाटीविलासिनी ।
महादुर्गा महोत्साहा महादेवबलोदया ॥ 122 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
ardhachandrōllasaddaṃṣṭrā yajñavāṭīvilāsinī ।
mahādurgā mahōtsāhā mahādēvabalōdayā ॥ 122 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Ardha-chandrollasad-damshtra (with fangs gleaming like a half-moon), Yajna-vati-vilasini (sporting in the sacrificial precinct), Mahadurga (great Durga), Mahotsaha (of great enthusiasm), Mahadeva-balodaya (arising from the strength of Mahadeva). (122)

contentBlocks.original:
डाकिनीड्या शाकिनीड्या साकिनीड्या समस्तजुट् ।
निरङ्कुशा नाकिवन्द्या षडाधाराधिदेवता ॥ 123 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
ḍākinīḍyā śākinīḍyā sākinīḍyā samastajuṭ ।
niraṅkuśā nākivandyā ṣaḍādhārādhidēvatā ॥ 123 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Dakinidhya (meditated upon by Dakinis), Shakinidhya (by Shakinis), Sakinidhya (by Sakinis), Samastajut (praised by all together), Nirankusha (without restraint), Nakivandya (worshipped in heaven), Shadadhara-adhidevata (presiding deity of the six support-chakras). (123)

contentBlocks.original:
भुवनज्ञानिनिश्श्रेणी भुवनाकारवल्लरी ।
शाश्वती शाश्वताकारा लोकानुग्रहकारिणी ॥ 124 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
bhuvanajñāniniśśrēṇī bhuvanākāravallarī ।
śāśvatī śāśvatākārā lōkānugrahakāriṇī ॥ 124 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Bhuvana-jñanini-shreni (series of world-knowers), Bhuvana-kara-vallari (creeper of world-form), Shashvati (eternal), Shashvata-kara (of eternal form), Lokanugrahakārinī (bestower of grace upon the worlds). (124)

contentBlocks.original:
सारसी मानसी हंसी हंसलोकप्रदायिनी ।
चिन्मुद्रालङ्कृतकरा कोटिसूर्यसमप्रभा ॥ 125 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
sārasī mānasī haṃsī haṃsalōkapradāyinī ।
chinmudrālaṅkṛtakarā kōṭisūryasamaprabhā ॥ 125 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Sarasi (dwelling in the lake), Manasi (mind-born), Hamsi (swan-formed), Hamsa-loka-pradayini (bestower of the world of the swan/liberated), Chinmudra-alankrita-kara (with hands adorned with the gesture of consciousness), Koti-surya-sama-prabha (radiant as millions of suns). (125)

contentBlocks.original:
सुखप्राणिशिरोरेखा सददृष्टप्रदायिनी ।
सर्वसाङ्कर्यदोषघ्नी ग्रहोपद्रवनाशिनी ॥ 126 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
sukhaprāṇiśirōrēkhā sadadṛṣṭapradāyinī ।
sarvasāṅkaryadōṣaghnī grahōpadravanāśinī ॥ 126 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Sukha-prani-shiro-rekha (auspicious line on the head of the living), Sadad-rishta-pradayini (giver of eternal good sight), Sarvasankaryad-oshajni (destroyer of the fault of all mixing), Grahopadrava-nashini (destroyer of planetary afflictions). (126)

contentBlocks.original:
क्षुद्रजन्तुभयघ्नी च विषरोगादिभञ्जनी ।
सदाशान्ता सदाशुद्धा गृहच्छिद्रनिवारिणी ॥ 127 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
kṣudrajantubhayaghnī cha viṣarōgādibhañjanī ।
sadāśāntā sadāśuddhā gṛhachChidranivāriṇī ॥ 127 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Kshudra-jantubhaya-ghni (destroyer of the fear of small creatures), Visharoga-adi-bhanjani (destroyer of poison, disease, and more), Sada-shanta (always peaceful), Sada-shuddha (always pure), Grihachchidra-nivarini (preventer of flaws in the home). (127)

contentBlocks.original:
कलिदोषप्रशमनी कोलाहलपुरस्थिता ।
गौरी लाक्षणिकी मुख्या जघन्याकृतिवर्जिता ॥ 128 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
kalidōṣapraśamanī kōlāhalapurasthitā ।
gaurī lākṣaṇikī mukhyā jaghanyākṛtivarjitā ॥ 128 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Kalidosha-prashamani (pacifier of the faults of the Kali age), Kolahala-pura-sthita (dwelling in the city of commotion), Gauri (the fair), Lakshnikī (the auspicious), Mukhya (the principal), Jaghanya-kriti-varjita (free from base forms). (128)

contentBlocks.original:
माया विद्या मूलभूता वासवी विष्णुचेतना ।
वादिनी वसुरूपा च वसुरत्नपरिच्छदा ॥ 129 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
māyā vidyā mūlabhūtā vāsavī viṣṇuchētanā ।
vādinī vasurūpā cha vasuratnaparichChadā ॥ 129 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Maya (illusion), Vidya (knowledge), Mula-bhuta (root-being), Vasavi (Indra's goddess), Vishnu-chetana (Vishnu's consciousness), Vadini (the speaker), Vasurupa (wealth-formed), Vasuratnapa-ricchhada (adorned with the covering of jewels of wealth). (129)

contentBlocks.original:
छान्दसी चन्द्रहृदया मन्त्रस्वच्छन्दभैरवी ।
वनमाला वैजयन्ती पञ्चदिव्यायुधात्मिका ॥ 130 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
Chāndasī chandrahṛdayā mantrasvachChandabhairavī ।
vanamālā vaijayantī pañchadivyāyudhātmikā ॥ 130 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Chhandasi (of the metres), Chandra-hridaya (moon-hearted), Mantra-svachanda-bhairavi (freely self-willed Bhairavi of mantras), Vanamala (forest-garlanded), Vaijayanti (the victory-garland), Pancha-divyayuddhatmika (essence of the five divine weapons). (130)

contentBlocks.original:
पीताम्बरमयी चञ्चत्कौस्तुभा हरिकामिनी ।
नित्या तथ्या रमा रामा रमणी मृत्युभञ्जनी ॥ 131 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
pītāmbaramayī chañchatkaustubhā harikāminī ।
nityā tathyā ramā rāmā ramaṇī mṛtyubhañjanī ॥ 131 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Pitambara-mayi (made of yellow silk), Chanchhat-kaustubha (with gleaming Kaustubha gem), Hari-kamini (Hari's beloved), Nitya (eternal), Tathya (truthful), Rama (Rama's consort/Lakshmi), Rama (Lakshmi), Ramanī (charming), Mrityu-bhanjani (destroyer of death). (131)

contentBlocks.original:
ज्येष्ठा काष्ठा धनिष्ठान्ता शराङ्गी निर्गुणप्रिया ।
मैत्रेया मित्रविन्दा च शेष्यशेषकलाशया ॥ 132 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
jyēṣṭhā kāṣṭhā dhaniṣṭhāntā śarāṅgī nirguṇapriyā ।
maitrēyā mitravindā cha śēṣyaśēṣakalāśayā ॥ 132 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Jyeshtha (eldest), Kashtha (the extreme), Dhanishthanta (ending with Dhanishtha star), Sharangi (bow-bearer), Nirguna-priya (beloved of the attributeless), Maitreya (Maitreyi's goddess), Mitravinda (friend-finder), Sheshya-shesha-kalashaya (dwelling in the remaining art of the remainder). (132)

contentBlocks.original:
वाराणसीवासलभ्या चार्यावर्तजनस्तुता । [ वाराणसीवासरता ]
जगदुत्पत्तिसंस्थानसंहारत्रयकारणम् ॥ 133 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
vārāṇasīvāsalabhyā chāryāvartajanastutā । [ vārāṇasīvāsaratā ]
jagadutpattisaṃsthānasaṃhāratrayakāraṇam ॥ 133 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Varanasi-vasa-labhya (attainable by dwelling in Varanasi), Aryavarta-janastuta (praised by the people of Aryavarta), Jagad-utpatti-samsthana-samhara-traya-karanam (the cause of the three — creation, sustenance, and dissolution of the world). (133)

contentBlocks.original:
त्वमम्ब विष्णुसर्वस्वं नमस्तेऽस्तु महेश्वरि ।
नमस्ते सर्वलोकानां जनन्यै पुण्यमूर्तये ॥ 134 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
tvamamba viṣṇusarvasvaṃ namastē'stu mahēśvari ।
namastē sarvalōkānāṃ jananyai puṇyamūrtayē ॥ 134 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: O Mother, you are the all of Vishnu. Salutations to you, O Maheshvari. Salutations to the mother of all the worlds, to the embodiment of merit. (134)

contentBlocks.original:
सिद्धलक्ष्मीर्महाकालि महलक्ष्मि नमोऽस्तु ते ।
सद्योजातादिपञ्चाग्निरूपा पञ्चकपञ्चकम् ॥ 135 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
siddhalakṣmīrmahākāḻi mahalakṣmi namō'stu tē ।
sadyōjātādipañchāgnirūpā pañchakapañchakam ॥ 135 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Salutations to Siddhalakshmi, Mahakali, Mahalakshmi. She who is the five fires of Sadyojata and others, the group of five-times-five — salutations to you. (135)

contentBlocks.original:
यन्त्रलक्ष्मीर्भवत्यादिराद्याद्ये ते नमो नमः ।
सृष्ट्यादिकारणाकारवितते दोषवर्जिते ॥ 136 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
yantralakṣmīrbhavatyādirādyādyē tē namō namaḥ ।
sṛṣṭyādikāraṇākāravitatē dōṣavarjitē ॥ 136 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Salutations again and again to Yantra-Lakshmi, to Bhavati and other primordial goddesses. O you who are the cause of creation and more, free of all faults. (136)

contentBlocks.original:
जगल्लक्ष्मीर्जगन्मातर्विष्णुपत्नि नमोऽस्तु ते ।
नवकोटिमहाशक्तिसमुपास्यपदाम्बुजे ॥ 137 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
jagallakṣmīrjaganmātarviṣṇupatni namō'stu tē ।
navakōṭimahāśaktisamupāsyapadāmbujē ॥ 137 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Salutations to you, Jagal-Lakshmi, O mother of the world, O consort of Vishnu. O you whose lotus feet are worshipped by nine crore great powers. (137)

contentBlocks.original:
कनत्सौवर्णरत्नाढ्य सर्वाभरणभूषिते ।
अनन्तानित्यमहिषीप्रपञ्चेश्वरनायकि ॥ 138 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
kanatsauvarṇaratnāḍhya sarvābharaṇabhūṣitē ।
anantānityamahiṣīprapañchēśvaranāyaki ॥ 138 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: O you who are rich in glittering golden jewels and adorned with all ornaments, O eternal and infinite queen, O sovereign of the entire manifest universe. (138)

contentBlocks.original:
अत्युच्छ्रितपदान्तस्थे परमव्योमनायकि ।
नाकपृष्ठगताराध्ये विष्णुलोकविलासिनि ॥ 139 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
atyuchChritapadāntasthē paramavyōmanāyaki ।
nākapṛṣṭhagatārādhyē viṣṇulōkavilāsini ॥ 139 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: O you who dwell at the highest end of the highest position, O sovereign of the supreme ether, O you who are worshipped on the roof of heaven, O you who delight in Vaishnava realms. (139)

contentBlocks.original:
वैकुण्ठराजमहिषि श्रीरङ्गनगराश्रिते ।
रङ्गनायकि भूपुत्रि कृष्णे वरदवल्लभे ॥ 140 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
vaikuṇṭharājamahiṣi śrīraṅganagarāśritē ।
raṅganāyaki bhūputri kṛṣṇē varadavallabhē ॥ 140 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: O queen of the Vaikuntha realm, O you who reside in the city of Srirangam, O sovereign of Srirangam, O daughter of the earth, O dark one, O beloved of the boon-giving lord. (140)

contentBlocks.original:
कोटिब्रह्मादिसंसेव्ये कोटिरुद्रादिकीर्तिते ।
मातुलुङ्गमयं खेटं सौवर्णचषकं तथा ॥ 141 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
kōṭibrahmādisaṃsēvyē kōṭirudrādikīrtitē ।
mātuluṅgamayaṃ khēṭaṃ sauvarṇachaṣakaṃ tathā ॥ 141 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: O you who are served by millions of Brahmas and extolled by millions of Rudras — a citrus fruit in the hand, a golden cup, (141)

contentBlocks.original:
पद्मद्वयं पूर्णकुम्भं कीरञ्च वरदाभये ।
पाशमङ्कुशकं शङ्खं चक्रं शूलं कृपाणिकाम् ॥ 142 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
padmadvayaṃ pūrṇakumbhaṃ kīrañcha varadābhayē ।
pāśamaṅkuśakaṃ śaṅkhaṃ chakraṃ śūlaṃ kṛpāṇikām ॥ 142 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Two lotuses, a full pot, a cockatoo, the gesture of boon-giving and protection, a noose, a goad, a conch, a discus, a trident, a sword, (142)

contentBlocks.original:
