धनुर्बाणौ चाक्षमालां चिन्मुद्रामपि बिभ्रती ।
अष्टादशभुजे लक्ष्मीर्महाष्टादशपीठगे ॥ 143 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
dhanurbāṇau chākṣamālāṃ chinmudrāmapi bibhratī ।
aṣṭādaśabhujē lakṣmīrmahāṣṭādaśapīṭhagē ॥ 143 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: A bow with arrow, and also a rosary and the gesture of consciousness — these she bears. O eighteen-armed Lakshmi, dwelling on the eighteen great seats. (143)

contentBlocks.original:
भूमिनीलादिसंसेव्ये स्वामिचित्तानुवर्तिनि ।
पद्मे पद्मालये पद्मि पूर्णकुम्भाभिषेचिते ॥ 144 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
bhūminīlādisaṃsēvyē svāmichittānuvartini ।
padmē padmālayē padmi pūrṇakumbhābhiṣēchitē ॥ 144 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: O you who are served by Bhumi, Nila, and others, who follow the will of your lord, O Padma (lotus), O she who dwells in the lotus, O lotus-holding one, O you consecrated by the pouring of a full pot. (144)

contentBlocks.original:
इन्दिरेन्दिन्दिराभाक्षि क्षीरसागरकन्यके ।
भार्गवि त्वं स्वतन्त्रेच्छा वशीकृतजगत्पतिः ॥ 145 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
indirēndindirābhākṣi kṣīrasāgarakanyakē ।
bhārgavi tvaṃ svatantrēchChā vaśīkṛtajagatpatiḥ ॥ 145 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: O Indira (goddess of beauty), with eyes like the blue lotus, O daughter of the milk-ocean, O Bhargavi (Lakshmi) — by your own free will you have brought even the lord of the universe under your sway. (145)

contentBlocks.original:
मङ्गलं मङ्गलानां त्वं देवतानां च देवता ।
त्वमुत्तमोत्तमानां च त्वं श्रेयः परमामृतम् ॥ 146 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
maṅgaḻaṃ maṅgaḻānāṃ tvaṃ dēvatānāṃ cha dēvatā ।
tvamuttamōttamānāṃ cha tvaṃ śrēyaḥ paramāmṛtam ॥ 146 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: You are the auspiciousness of all auspicious things, the deity of all deities. You are the most excellent of the most excellent; you are the supreme good, the immortal nectar. (146)

contentBlocks.original:
धनधान्याभिवृद्धिश्च सार्वभौमसुखोच्छ्रया ।
आन्दोलिकादिसौभाग्यं मत्तेभादिमहोदयः ॥ 147 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
dhanadhānyābhivṛddhiścha sārvabhaumasukhōchChrayā ।
āndōḻikādisaubhāgyaṃ mattēbhādimahōdayaḥ ॥ 147 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Growth of wealth and grain, the lofty joy of universal sovereignty, the fortune of palanquins and such, the greatness of elephants in rut — (147)

contentBlocks.original:
पुत्रपौत्राभिवृद्धिश्च विद्याभोगबलादिकम् ।
आयुरारोग्यसम्पत्तिरष्टैश्वर्यं त्वमेव हि ॥ 148 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
putrapautrābhivṛddhiścha vidyābhōgabalādikam ।
āyurārōgyasampattiraṣṭaiśvaryaṃ tvamēva hi ॥ 148 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Growth of sons and grandsons, knowledge, enjoyment, and strength, longevity, health, and prosperity, the eightfold sovereignty — all of this you are yourself. (148)

contentBlocks.original:
परमेशविभूतिश्च सूक्ष्मात्सूक्ष्मतरागतिः ।
सदयापाङ्गसन्दत्तब्रह्मेन्द्रादिपदस्थितिः ॥ 149 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
paramēśavibhūtiścha sūkṣmātsūkṣmatarāgatiḥ ।
sadayāpāṅgasandattabrahmēndrādipadasthitiḥ ॥ 149 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: The divine power of the supreme lord, the path subtler than the subtlest, the firm establishment in the positions of Brahma, Indra, and others, granted by your compassionate sidelong glance. (149)

contentBlocks.original:
अव्याहतमहाभाग्यं त्वमेवाक्षोभ्यविक्रमः ।
समन्वयश्च वेदानामविरोधस्त्वमेव हि ॥ 150 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
avyāhatamahābhāgyaṃ tvamēvākṣōbhyavikramaḥ ।
samanvayaścha vēdānāmavirōdhastvamēva hi ॥ 150 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Unimpeded great fortune — you yourself are that invincible valour. The harmony of the Vedas, their freedom from contradiction — you alone are that. (150)

contentBlocks.original:
निःश्रेयसपदप्राप्तिसाधनं फलमेव च ।
श्रीमन्त्रराजराज्ञी च श्रीविद्या क्षेमकारिणी ॥ 151 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
niḥśrēyasapadaprāptisādhanaṃ phalamēva cha ।
śrīmantrarājarājñī cha śrīvidyā kṣēmakāriṇī ॥ 151 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: The means of attaining the highest good, and the fruit itself — O queen of the royal Sri Mantra, O Sri Vidya, O bestower of welfare. (151)

contentBlocks.original:
श्रीम्बीजजपसन्तुष्टा ऐं ह्रीं श्रीं बीजपालिका ।
प्रपत्तिमार्गसुलभा विष्णुप्रथमकिङ्करी ॥ 152 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
śrīmbījajapasantuṣṭā aiṃ hrīṃ śrīṃ bījapālikā ।
prapattimārgasulabhā viṣṇuprathamakiṅkarī ॥ 152 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: O she who is pleased by the chanting of the Shrim seed-syllable, O keeper of the Aim-Hrim-Shrim seed syllables, O she who is easily accessible through the path of surrender, O first servant of Vishnu. (152)

contentBlocks.original:
क्लीङ्कारार्थसवित्री च सौमङ्गल्याधिदेवता ।
श्रीषोडशाक्षरीविद्या श्रीयन्त्रपुरवासिनी ॥ 153 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
klīṅkārārthasavitrī cha saumaṅgaḻyādhidēvatā ।
śrīṣōḍaśākṣarīvidyā śrīyantrapuravāsinī ॥ 153 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: O Savitrī of the meaning of the Klīm syllable, O presiding deity of all auspiciousness, O Sri Shodashakshari Vidya (sixteen-syllable knowledge of Sri), O you who dwell in the Sri Yantra-city. (153)

contentBlocks.original:
सर्वमङ्गलमाङ्गल्ये शिवे सर्वार्थसाधिके ।
शरण्ये त्र्यम्बके गौरी नारायणि नमोऽस्तु ते ॥ 154 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
sarvamaṅgaḻamāṅgaḻyē śivē sarvārthasādhikē ।
śaraṇyē tryambakē gaurī nārāyaṇi namō'stu tē ॥ 154 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: O she who is the auspicious among all auspicious things, the auspicious Shiva, the fulfiller of all purposes, the refuge, the three-eyed Gauri, O Narayani — salutations to you. (154)

contentBlocks.original:
पुनः पुनर्नमस्तेऽस्तु साष्टाङ्गमयुतं पुनः ।
सनत्कुमार उवाच-
एवं स्तुता महालक्ष्मीर्ब्रह्मरुद्रादिभिस्सुरैः ।
नमद्भिरार्तैर्दीनैश्च निस्स्वत्वैर्भोगवर्जितैः ॥ 1 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
punaḥ punarnamastē'stu sāṣṭāṅgamayutaṃ punaḥ ।
sanatkumāra uvācha-
ēvaṃ stutā mahālakṣmīrbrahmarudrādibhissuraiḥ ।
namadbhirārtairdīnaiścha nissvatvairbhōgavarjitaiḥ ॥ 1 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Again and again salutations to you, along with prostration of all eight limbs, again and again. Sanatkumara said: Thus was Mahalakshmi praised by Brahma, Rudra, and all the gods, bowing humbly, distressed, poor, devoid of enjoyment. (1)

contentBlocks.original:
ज्येष्ठा जुष्टैश्च निश्श्रीकैस्संसारात्स्वपरायणैः ।
विष्णुपत्नी ददौ तेषां दर्शनं दृष्टितर्पणम् ॥ 2 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
jyēṣṭhā juṣṭaiścha niśśrīkaissaṃsārātsvaparāyaṇaiḥ ।
viṣṇupatnī dadau tēṣāṃ darśanaṃ dṛṣṭitarpaṇam ॥ 2 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: And by the decrepit, the ill-fortuned, those devoted to crossing the ocean of existence — Vishnupatni (Lakshmi, consort of Vishnu) gave them the vision that satisfies the eyes. (2)

contentBlocks.original:
शरत्पूर्णेन्दुकोट्याभधवलापाङ्गवीक्षणैः ।
सर्वान्सत्त्वसमाविष्टान् चक्रे हृष्टा वरं ददौ ॥ 3 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
śaratpūrṇēndukōṭyābhadhavaḻāpāṅgavīkṣaṇaiḥ ।
sarvānsattvasamāviṣṭān chakrē hṛṣṭā varaṃ dadau ॥ 3 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: With sidelong glances white and pure as millions of autumn full moons, she entered all of them with sattva, was delighted, and bestowed boons. (3)

contentBlocks.original:
महालक्ष्मीरुवाच-
नाम्नां साष्टसहस्रं मे प्रमादाद्वापि यस्सकृत् ।
कीर्तयेत्तत्कुले सत्यं वसाम्याचन्द्रतारकम् ॥ 4 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
mahālakṣmīruvācha-
nāmnāṃ sāṣṭasahasraṃ mē pramādādvāpi yassakṛt ।
kīrtayēttatkulē satyaṃ vasāmyāchandratārakam ॥ 4 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Mahalakshmi said: Whoever, even carelessly, once recites my eight thousand names — in that family I shall truly dwell for as long as the moon and stars endure. (4)

contentBlocks.original:
किं पुनर्नियमाज्जप्तुर्मदेकशरणस्य च ।
मातृवत्सानुकम्पाहं पोषकी स्यामहर्निशम् ॥ 5 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
kiṃ punarniyamājjapturmadēkaśaraṇasya cha ।
mātṛvatsānukampāhaṃ pōṣakī syāmaharniśam ॥ 5 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: What more need be said of one who chants with devotion, surrendered to me alone — like a mother full of compassion, I shall nourish him day and night. (5)

contentBlocks.original:
मन्नाम स्तवतां लोके दुर्लभं नास्ति चिन्तितम् ।
मत्प्रसादेन सर्वेऽपि स्वस्वेष्टार्थमवाप्स्यथ ॥ 6 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
mannāma stavatāṃ lōkē durlabhaṃ nāsti chintitam ।
matprasādēna sarvē'pi svasvēṣṭārthamavāpsyatha ॥ 6 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: For those who praise my names in this world, nothing desired is difficult to obtain. By my grace, all of them shall attain their own respective desired objects. (6)

contentBlocks.original:
लुप्तवैष्णवधर्मस्य मद्व्रतेष्ववकीर्णिनः ।
भक्तिप्रपत्तिहीनस्य वन्द्यो नाम्नां स्तवोऽपि मे ॥ 7 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
luptavaiṣṇavadharmasya madvratēṣvavakīrṇinaḥ ।
bhaktiprapattihīnasya vandyō nāmnāṃ stavō'pi mē ॥ 7 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Even for one whose Vaishnava dharma has lapsed, who has transgressed my vows, who is devoid of devotion and surrender — even such a person's praise of my names is worthy of reverence by me. (7)

contentBlocks.original:
तस्मादवश्यं तैर्दोषैर्विहीनः पापवर्जितः ।
जपेत्साष्टसहस्रं मे नाम्नां प्रत्यहमादरात् ॥ 8 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
tasmādavaśyaṃ tairdōṣairvihīnaḥ pāpavarjitaḥ ।
japētsāṣṭasahasraṃ mē nāmnāṃ pratyahamādarāt ॥ 8 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Therefore one who is freed from such faults and is rid of sins should chant my eight thousand names reverently each day. (8)

contentBlocks.original:
साक्षादलक्ष्मीपुत्रोऽपि दुर्भाग्योऽप्यलसोऽपि वा ।
अप्रयत्नोऽपि मूढोऽपि विकलः पतितोऽपि च ॥ 9 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
sākṣādalakṣmīputrō'pi durbhāgyō'pyalasō'pi vā ।
aprayatnō'pi mūḍhō'pi vikalaḥ patitō'pi cha ॥ 9 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Even one who is the very son of Alakshmi (ill-fortune), even the unfortunate, the indolent, the effortless, the foolish, the incomplete, and the fallen — (9)

contentBlocks.original:
अवश्यं प्राप्नुयाद्भाग्यं मत्प्रसादेन केवलम् ।
स्पृहेयमचिराद्देवा वरदानाय जापिनः ।
ददामि सर्वमिष्टार्थं लक्ष्मीति स्मरतां ध्रुवम् ॥ 10 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
avaśyaṃ prāpnuyādbhāgyaṃ matprasādēna kēvalam ।
spṛhēyamachirāddēvā varadānāya jāpinaḥ ।
dadāmi sarvamiṣṭārthaṃ lakṣmīti smaratāṃ dhruvam ॥ 10 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: By my grace alone, he shall certainly attain fortune. The gods themselves shall soon yearn to bestow boons on the one who chants. I shall grant all desired objects to those who remember Lakshmi — this is certain. (10)

contentBlocks.original:
सनत्कुमार उवाच-
इत्युक्त्वाऽन्तर्दधे लक्ष्मीर्वैष्णवी भगवत्कला ।
इष्टापूर्तं च सुकृतं भागधेयं च चिन्तितम् ॥ 11 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
sanatkumāra uvācha-
ityuktvā'ntardadhē lakṣmīrvaiṣṇavī bhagavatkalā ।
iṣṭāpūrtaṃ cha sukṛtaṃ bhāgadhēyaṃ cha chintitam ॥ 11 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Sanatkumara said: Having spoken thus, Lakshmi — the divine power of Vishnu — disappeared. All that was wished for, all merit, all good fortune, all that was desired (11)

contentBlocks.original:
स्वं स्वं स्थानं च भोगं च विजयं लेभिरे सुराः ।
तदेतत् प्रवदाम्यद्य लक्ष्मीनामसहस्रकम् ।
योगिनः पठत क्षिप्रं चिन्तितार्थानवाप्स्यथ ॥ 12 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
svaṃ svaṃ sthānaṃ cha bhōgaṃ cha vijayaṃ lēbhirē surāḥ ।
tadētat pravadāmyadya lakṣmīnāmasahasrakam ।
yōginaḥ paṭhata kṣipraṃ chintitārthānavāpsyatha ॥ 12 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: along with their own places, enjoyments, and victories — all the gods obtained. I now recount to you this Thousand-Names of Lakshmi. O yogis, recite it quickly and you shall obtain all desired objects. (12)

contentBlocks.original:
गार्ग्य उवाच-
सनत्कुमारोयोगीन्द्र इत्युक्त्वा स दयानिधिः ।
अनुगृह्य ययौ क्षिप्रं तांश्च द्वादशयोगिनः ॥ 13 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
gārgya uvācha-
sanatkumārōyōgīndra ityuktvā sa dayānidhiḥ ।
anugṛhya yayau kṣipraṃ tāṃścha dvādaśayōginaḥ ॥ 13 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Gargya said: The compassionate sage Sanatkumara, the chief of yogis, having spoken thus and blessed them, quickly departed, along with those twelve yogis. (13)

contentBlocks.original:
तस्मादेतद्रहस्यं च गोप्यं जप्यं प्रयत्नतः ।
अष्टम्यां च चतुर्दश्यां नवम्यां भृगुवासरे ॥ 14 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
tasmādētadrahasyaṃ cha gōpyaṃ japyaṃ prayatnataḥ ।
aṣṭamyāṃ cha chaturdaśyāṃ navamyāṃ bhṛguvāsarē ॥ 14 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Therefore this secret teaching is to be kept confidential and chanted with great effort — on the eighth, fourteenth, or ninth lunar day, or on a Friday, (14)

contentBlocks.original:
पौर्णमास्याममायां च पर्वकाले विशेषतः ।
जपेद्वा नित्यकार्येषु सर्वान्कामानवाप्नुयात् ॥ 15 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
paurṇamāsyāmamāyāṃ cha parvakālē viśēṣataḥ ।
japēdvā nityakāryēṣu sarvānkāmānavāpnuyāt ॥ 15 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: on the full-moon day, the new-moon day, or especially during sacred junction times — or in daily practice: whoever chants it shall obtain all desired objects. (15)

contentBlocks.original: इति श्रीस्कन्दपुराणे सनत्कुमारसंहितायां लक्ष्मीसहस्रनामस्तोत्रं सम्पूर्णम् ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration: iti śrīskandapurāṇē sanatkumārasaṃhitāyāṃ lakṣmīsahasranāmastōtraṃ sampūrṇam ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Thus ends the Sri Lakshmi Sahasranama Stotram from the Sanatkumara Samhita of the Sri Skanda Purana.
